How to Check MPPSC State Engineering Service Result 2026?

Candidates can download their MPPSC State Engineering Service Result 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website mppsc.mp.gov.in
  2. Click on the "Result" or "What's New" section
  3. Find and click on the Written Exam Result - State Engineering Service Exam 2025 link
  4. Download the PDF merit list
  5. Search your Roll Number using Ctrl+F

MPPSC State Engineering Service Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The MPPSC State Engineering Service merit list 2026 is a comprehensive document containing details of all qualified candidates. MPPSC prepares separate merit lists for different categories.

Merit List Contains:

  • Roll Number of qualified candidates
  • Candidate Name (as per application)
  • Father's/Mother's Name
  • Category (Gen/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Total Marks Obtained
  • Final Rank in merit
  • Qualifying Status (Qualified/Not Qualified)

Types of Merit Lists Released:

  1. General Merit List: Overall toppers regardless of category
  2. Category-Wise Merit List: Separate lists for OBC, SC, ST, EWS candidates
  3. Waiting List: Candidates on waitlist if selected candidates withdraw

Your MPPSC State Engineering Service scorecard 2026 will display the following information:

  • Start preparing for Interview/Document Verification
  • Keep all original documents ready
  • Verify personal details on scorecard - report discrepancies within stipulated time

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Key Highlights

  • Advertisement No.: 03/2026, issued by Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB) dated 29/05/2026.
  • Total Vacancies: 1,979 posts across 25 post codes in multiple departments of Government of NCT of Delhi.
  • Departments: Forensic Science Laboratory, Directorate of Education, New Delhi Municipal Council (NDMC), Information Technology Department, Department of Delhi Archives, and Labour Department.
  • Apply Mode: Online only at https://dsssbonline.nic.in.
  • Application Opens: 16 June 2026 (from 12:00 Noon).
  • Last Date to Apply: 15 July 2026 (till 11:59 PM).
  • Application Fee: ₹100/-. Women, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen are exempted.
  • Qualification: Ranges from Matric/ITI (for technical posts) to Master's degree (for scientific/teaching posts) depending on the post.
  • Age Limit: Varies by post - generally 18–27 years for scientific posts, up to 30–32 years for technical/teaching posts. Age relaxation applicable as per Central Government rules.
  • Exam Type: One Tier (General) or One Tier (Technical/Teaching) Computer Based Test of 200 marks. Negative marking of 0.25 marks per wrong answer.
  • Minimum Qualifying Marks: General/EWS: 40%, OBC (Delhi): 35%, SC/ST/PwBD: 30%.

The Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB) has released Advertisement No. 03/2026, inviting online applications from eligible candidates for recruitment to 1,979 posts across 25 different post codes in various departments and autonomous bodies of the Government of NCT of Delhi. This is one of the largest DSSSB recruitment cycles in 2026, covering a wide range of profiles - from Junior Scientific Assistants in the Forensic Science Laboratory to Trained Graduate Teachers (Computer Science) in the Directorate of Education, and technical posts such as Fitter, Lift Operator, Sub Station Apprentice, and Electrical Overseer in NDMC and the Labour Department. The notification was issued on 29 May 2026 and the online application window opens on 16 June 2026.

All posts are permanent government positions under the Government of NCT of Delhi or its autonomous and local bodies. Candidates can apply online only through the DSSSB portal at dsssbonline.nic.in. The closing date for all applications is 15 July 2026. Eligibility criteria - including age, educational qualification, and experience - will be determined as on the closing date of application, i.e., 15 July 2026.

FOLLOW US

DSSSB Recruitment 2026 Advertisement No. 03/2026 - Overview

DSSSB Advertisement 03/2026 covers 25 post codes under six major departments of the Government of NCT of Delhi. The total vacancy count of 1,979 posts includes reservations for UR, OBC (Delhi), SC, ST, EWS, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen (ESM), and Meritorious Sports Persons (MSP) categories.

DSSSB 03/2026 Important Dates

DSSSB 03/2026 Vacancy Details - All Post Codes

DSSSB Advertisement No. 03/2026 covers a total of 1,979 vacancies spread across 25 post codes. The largest share of vacancies belongs to the Directorate of Education - with 675 posts for Trained Graduate Teacher (Computer Science), 450 posts for Special Educator (Primary), 163 posts for TGT (Special Education Teacher), and 129 posts for Domestic Science Teacher. In the Forensic Science Laboratory, vacancies span 11 different specializations of the Junior Scientific Assistant role, with the highest number in Cyber Forensic (81 posts) and Crime Scene/District Mobile Forensic Unit (46 posts). The Information Technology Department has 125 posts for IT Assistant Grade-A, which is the largest single technical vacancy outside of teaching. NDMC is recruiting for Fitter Grade-II, Lift Operator, Sub Station Apprentice, Assistant Operator cum Mechanic (A/C), Data Processing Assistant, and Data Entry Operator roles.

DSSSB 03/2026 - Post-wise Eligibility, Qualification & Pay Scale

Eligibility requirements differ significantly across the 25 post codes in this advertisement. For all 11 Junior Scientific Assistant posts in the Forensic Science Laboratory (post codes 21/26 to 31/26), the minimum educational qualification is a Master's degree in the relevant science subject from a recognized university, and the age limit is 18 to 27 years with a pay scale of ₹29,200 – ₹92,300 (Pay Level 5). For teaching posts in the Directorate of Education (post codes 41/26 to 44/26), graduation with B.Ed or a relevant Diploma in Special Education is required along with CTET qualification wherever applicable, and the pay scale ranges from ₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400 to ₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400 depending on the post group. For technical posts in NDMC and the Labour Department, ITI certificates or Diplomas in the relevant trade are required. The IT Assistant Grade-A post (34/26) requires only a Class 12 pass with a typing speed of 8,000 key depressions per hour. All newly recruited candidates must undergo a mandatory induction training of at least two weeks as a prerequisite for probation completion.

40/26 Data Entry Operator Grade 'B' Graduation / 3-year Diploma in Computer/Digital Electronics/ECE AND typing speed of 8,000 key depressions per hour AND qualify aptitude test Below 30 years ₹29,200 – 92,300 (Level 5)
41/26 TGT (Computer Science) BCA / Graduation in Computer Science (CS as main subject all years) / BE/B.Tech (CS/IT) / Graduation in any subject with DOEACC 'A' Level Certificate Up to 30 years ₹44,900 – 1,42,400 (Level 7)
42/26 Domestic Science Teacher Bachelor's degree in Domestic Science / Home Science AND B.Ed with Domestic Science / Home Science as teaching subject. Desirable: Hindi at secondary level. Up to 30 years ₹44,900 – 1,42,400 (Level 7)
43/26 TGT (Special Education Teacher) Graduation AND B.Ed (Special Education) / B.Ed General + Diploma/PG Diploma in Special Education / or equivalent RCI-recognized qualification AND CTET (Elementary Stage) AND RCI Registration Up to 30 years ₹44,900 – 1,42,400 (Level 7)
44/26 Special Educator (Primary) Class 12 AND 2-year D.Ed Special Education / 1-year DSE / DCBR + 6-month certificate / or equivalent RCI-recognized qualification AND CTET (Primary Stage) AND RCI Registration Up to 30 years ₹35,400 – 1,12,400 (Level 6)
45/26 Electrical Overseer / Sub-Inspector 3-year Diploma or Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering AND 1 year experience (for Diploma holders) in Electrical Engineering / power generation / transmission / distribution 18–27 years ₹29,200 – 92,300 (Level 5)

DSSSB 03/2026 - Age Limit & Age Relaxation

Age limits for posts in DSSSB Advertisement 03/2026 vary by post. Most Junior Scientific Assistant posts (FSL) have an age limit of 18 to 27 years. Teaching posts in the Directorate of Education have an upper age limit of 30 years. Technical posts in NDMC vary between 18–32 years and 18–37 years depending on the post. The eligibility for age will be determined as on the closing date of application, 15 July 2026. Age relaxation is available for SC/ST (5 years), OBC-Delhi (3 years), PwBD (10 years), Ex-Servicemen (military service period plus 3 years), and departmental candidates (up to 40 years for Group C posts in the same line). Contractual employees of the same department may also claim one-time age relaxation up to 5 years, subject to having worked at least 180 working days (or 120 days for teaching posts) in that particular year.

S.No. Category Age Relaxation
1 SC/ST (Group B & C) 5 years
2 OBC-Delhi (Group B & C) 3 years
3 PwBD (Group B & C) 10 years
4 PwBD + SC/ST (Group B & C) 15 years
5 PwBD + OBC (Group B & C) 13 years
6 Departmental Candidate (Regular Govt. servant with min. 3 years continuous service) - Group C post Up to 40 years (45 for SC/ST; 43 for OBC)
7 Ex-Servicemen (Group B & C Non-Gazetted) Military service period + 3 years (max. 55 years)
8 Meritorious Sports Persons (Group C only) 5 years (10 for SC/ST; 8 for OBC)
9 Disabled Defence Service Personnel (Group C only) 45 years (50 for SC/ST; 48 for OBC)
10 Widows / Divorced Women / Women judicially separated (Group C only) Up to 35 years (40 for SC/ST; 38 for OBC)

DSSSB 03/2026 Application Fee

Category Application Fee
General (UR) / OBC / EWS Male Candidates ₹100/- (via SBI e-Pay only)
Women Candidates Exempted (No Fee)
SC / ST Candidates Exempted (No Fee)
PwBD Candidates Exempted (No Fee)
Ex-Servicemen (ESM) Exempted (No Fee) - Note: ESM already re-employed in regular Central/Delhi Govt. post are NOT eligible for fee exemption

Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. Payment must be made through SBI e-Pay only. No other mode of payment will be accepted.

DSSSB 03/2026 Examination Scheme & Selection Process

Selection for all posts in DSSSB Advertisement 03/2026 will be through a Computer Based Test (CBT). The exam scheme is divided into three types depending on the post. Post codes 34/26, 39/26, and 40/26 (IT Assistant Grade-A, Data Processing Assistant, Data Entry Operator) will be examined under the One Tier General (I-T-G) scheme - a 2-hour paper of 200 MCQs covering General Awareness, Reasoning, Numerical Ability, Hindi, and English (40 marks each). All Forensic Science Laboratory posts, NDMC technical posts, Delhi Archives posts, and the Electrical Overseer post (i.e., post codes 21/26 to 33/26, 35/26 to 38/26, and 45/26) will follow the One Tier Technical (I-T-T) scheme - a 2-hour paper split into Section A (100 MCQs on general subjects, 100 marks) and Section B (100 MCQs on the domain subject, 100 marks). Teaching posts - TGT Computer Science, Domestic Science Teacher, TGT Special Education, and Special Educator Primary (post codes 41/26 to 44/26) - follow the One Tier Technical/Teaching scheme with the same structure, where Section B includes questions on teaching methodology and B.Ed syllabus.

Negative marking of 0.25 marks applies for every wrong MCQ answer. Questions will be bilingual (Hindi and English), except language-specific papers. The minimum qualifying marks are 40% for General/EWS, 35% for OBC (Delhi), and 30% for SC/ST/PwBD. Ex-Servicemen get an additional 5% relaxation in their respective categories, subject to a minimum of 30%.

Scheme Post Codes Duration Total Questions Total Marks Sections
One Tier General (I-T-G) 34/26, 39/26, 40/26 2 Hours 200 MCQs 200 5 sections: General Awareness, Reasoning, Numerical Ability, Hindi, English (40 marks each)
One Tier Technical (I-T-T) 21–33/26, 35–38/26, 45/26 2 Hours 200 MCQs 200 Section A: General subjects (100 marks) | Section B: Subject-specific domain (100 marks)
One Tier Technical/Teaching (I-T-T) 41/26, 42/26, 43/26, 44/26 2 Hours 200 MCQs 200 Section A: General subjects (100 marks) | Section B: Subject + Teaching Methodology/B.Ed (100 marks)

 

Category Minimum Qualifying Marks
General / EWS 40%
OBC (Delhi) 35%
SC / ST / PwBD 30%
Ex-Servicemen 5% relaxation in respective category, subject to minimum 30%

How to Apply for DSSSB Recruitment 2026 (Advertisement No. 03/2026)

Candidates must apply online only through the official DSSSB portal at dsssbonline.nic.in. Before applying, candidates must complete a one-time registration on the portal. The same user ID and password can be used for all future DSSSB examinations - no separate registration is required for each post. However, only one online application is allowed per post code; if multiple applications are detected for the same post code, all will be rejected and the candidate may be debarred. Once an application is submitted, no corrections or modifications - including change of category - will be permitted under any circumstances.

Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date of 15 July 2026 to avoid last-minute technical issues. The application fee of ₹100 must be paid through SBI e-Pay; no other payment mode is accepted. After successful submission, candidates should download and save a printout of their application for future reference. All supporting documents (educational certificates, caste certificates, experience certificates, etc.) will be required at the time of e-dossier upload, which will be called separately after the written examination. Candidates who fail to upload their e-dossier within the given time will have their candidature cancelled with no further opportunity.

  • Visit the DSSSB portal: https://dsssbonline.nic.in
  • Complete one-time registration (if not already registered).
  • Login and select the relevant post code from Advertisement No. 03/2026.
  • Fill in all details carefully - no changes can be made after submission.
  • Pay the application fee of ₹100 via SBI e-Pay (exempted for Women/SC/ST/PwBD/ESM).
  • Upload a recent colour passport photo (plain white/off-white background, clear face, eyes open) and signature.
  • Submit and download the application form printout.
  • Exam date will be announced separately on the DSSSB website.

DSSSB 03/2026 - Reservation & OBC Certificate Rules

Reservation benefits are available for SC, ST, OBC (Delhi), EWS, PwBD, ESM, and Meritorious Sports Persons as per Government of NCT of Delhi and Central Government instructions. An important point for OBC candidates: only OBC (Delhi) candidates notified by the Government of NCT of Delhi are eligible for reservation. OBC candidates from outside Delhi will be treated as Unreserved (UR) candidates and must apply under the UR category.

OBC (Delhi) candidates must possess a valid OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) certificate issued on or after 01/04/2026 but not later than 15/07/2026 (the closing date). For EWS candidates, an Income & Asset certificate valid for financial year 2026–27, based on income of financial year 2025–26, is required. The reservation roster, earmarking of vacancies, and PwBD post suitability are determined entirely by the respective User Departments, not by DSSSB.

DSSSB 03/2026 - Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Only one registration is allowed per candidate on the DSSSB portal. Multiple registrations will lead to cancellation of all applications.
  • Only one application per post code is allowed. Multiple applications for the same post code will result in cancellation and debarment.
  • The eligibility cut-off date is 15 July 2026 - age, qualification, experience, and all other eligibility conditions will be determined as on this date.
  • DSSSB does not verify eligibility at the application stage; candidature is accepted on a provisional basis. Verification happens at the e-dossier stage after the written examination.
  • No calculator, mobile phone, Bluetooth device, earbud, camera, smartwatch, or any other electronic gadget is allowed in the examination hall.
  • Dress code: light clothes with half sleeves, no big buttons, brooch, or badge; salwar/trouser; slippers or sandals with low heels. Shoes are not allowed.
  • Exam centres will be in Delhi/NCR and other states as decided by DSSSB.
  • DSSSB reserves the right to cancel, modify, or amend any part of the advertisement or examination at any stage.
  • In case of any discrepancy between the English, Hindi, Urdu, and Punjabi versions of the advertisement, the English version will be treated as final.
  • Candidates must regularly check the DSSSB website at https://dsssb.delhi.gov.in for updates and notifications/corrigenda.

In summary, DSSSB Advertisement No. 03/2026 offers 1,979 government job vacancies across 25 post codes in Delhi. The largest vacancy categories are TGT Computer Science (675 posts) in the Directorate of Education, Special Educator Primary (450 posts), JSA Cyber Forensic (81 posts) in the Forensic Science Laboratory, and IT Assistant Grade-A (125 posts) in the Information Technology Department. Candidates from a wide range of educational backgrounds - Class 12 pass to Master's degree holders - can find suitable posts in this advertisement. The application window is open from 16 June 2026 to 15 July 2026 and all candidates must apply online at dsssbonline.nic.in. The written examination, which will be a Computer Based Test, will be scheduled and notified separately on the DSSSB website at dsssb.delhi.gov.in.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has published the Combined Graduate Level Examination Notification 2026 on 21 May 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill approximately 12,256 Group B and Group C posts across various Ministries and Departments. Candidates within the age range as specified in the official notification and holding a Graduation Degree are eligible to apply online at https://ssc.gov.in. The application process will be active from 21 May 2026 to 22 June 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

FOLLOW US

SSC CGL 2026 Notification PDF

Staff Selection Commission (SSC), Block No. 12, CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003, has issued the official notice for Combined Graduate Level Examination, 2026 vide F. No. HQ-C11018/1/2026-C-1 dated 21-05-2026.The notification includes complete information on vacancies, eligibility, age limits, reservation, examination scheme, syllabus, exam centres, application process, and candidate instructions. Applicants should read the official notice carefully before submitting the form.

  • Download SSC CGL 2026 Official Notification PDF

SSC CGL 2026 Overview

SSC is conducting Combined Graduate Level Examination 2026 for approximately 12,256 vacancies in Group B and Group C posts under different Ministries, Departments, and Government of India organizations. The application process is online only, and the last date to submit the application form is 22 June 2026.

Examination Name Combined Graduate Level Examination, 2026 (SSC CGL 2026)
Advertisement No. F. No. HQ-C11018/1/2026-C-1 dated 21-05-2026
Post Name Various Group B (Gazetted/Non-Gazetted) and Group C posts including Assistant Section Officer, Inspector (IT/CE), Sub Inspector (CBI/NIA), Junior Statistical Officer, Assistant Audit Officer, Tax Assistant, Auditor, Accountant, Postal Assistant, and more
Total Vacancies Approximately 12,256 (tentative; updated vacancies will be on https://ssc.gov.in)

SSC CGL 2026 – Eligibility Criteria

Criteria Details
Nationality Citizen of India, or subjects/persons of specified neighboring and migrated countries as permitted under Government of India rules, with eligibility certificate wherever required.
Essential Educational Qualification Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University or equivalent for most posts, as on 01-08-2026.
Junior Statistical Officer (JSO) Bachelor's Degree in any subject with at least 60% marks in Mathematics at 12th level; or Bachelor's Degree with Statistics as one of the subjects at degree level.
Statistical Investigator Grade-II Bachelor's Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Demography, Population Studies, Operation Research, IT, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Data Science, or Artificial Intelligence.
Assistant Audit Officer / Assistant Accounts Officer Bachelor's Degree is essential. Desirable qualifications include CA, CMA, CS, M.Com, MBA (Finance), or M.B.E.
Appearing Candidates Final year candidates may apply, but they must possess the essential qualification on or before 01-08-2026.
Distance Learning Degrees Accepted only if approved by Distance Education Bureau, UGC, for Central Government employment. Proof must be produced at document verification.
BRO Posts Only male candidates are eligible for vacancies reported in Border Roads Organization.

SSC CGL 2026 – Age Limit

Age will be calculated as on 01-08-2026. The date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or Secondary Examination Certificate will be treated as final.

SSC CGL 2026 – Application Fee

Category Fee
General / OBC / EWS (Male) Rs. 100/- (Non-Refundable)
Women Candidates (All categories) NIL (Exempted)
SC / ST NIL (Exempted)
PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) NIL (Exempted)
Ex-Servicemen (ESM) eligible for reservation NIL (Exempted)

Fee must be paid online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or Visa, Mastercard, Maestro, and RuPay debit cards. Fee once paid will not be refunded, and applications without required fee payment will be rejected.

Correction Window Fee: Rs. 200/- for first correction and Rs. 500/- for second correction. This applies to all candidates, including fee-exempt applicants.

SSC CGL 2026 – Selection Process

Selection is through a two-tier Computer Based Examination (CBE). Marks scored in CBE conducted in multiple shifts will be normalized as per the procedure published by SSC vide Notice dated 02-06-2025. There is no provision for re-evaluation/re-checking of scores at any stage.

Tier-I: Computer Based Examination

Subject No. of Questions Maximum Marks Time
A. General Intelligence & Reasoning 25 50 1 hour (Sectional timer: 15 min per section)
1 hour 20 min for scribe-eligible PwBD candidates
B. General Awareness 25 50
C. Quantitative Aptitude 25 50
D. English Comprehension 25 50
Total 100 200  

Tier-I consists of Objective Type / MCQ questions, set in English and Hindi (except English Comprehension). Negative marking: 0.50 marks per wrong answer.

Tier-II: Computer Based Examination

Tier-II is conducted in Paper-I, Paper-II, and Paper-III in separate shifts/days.

Paper Section / Subject Questions Marks Duration
Paper-I (Compulsory for all posts) Section-I: Mathematical Abilities + Reasoning & General Intelligence 30+30 = 60 180 Session-I: 2 hrs 15 min; Section-I: 1 hr timer
Section-II: English Language & Comprehension + General Awareness 45+25 = 70 210 1 hr timer
Section-III: Computer Knowledge Test (qualifying, mandatory) 20 60 15 min
Section-IV: Data Entry Speed Test (DEST) (qualifying, mandatory) - - Session-II: 15 min
Paper-II (JSO / Statistical Investigator only) Statistics 100 200 2 hours
Paper-III (AAO / AAcO only) General Studies (Finance and Economics) 100 200 2 hours

Negative marking in Tier-II: 1 mark per wrong answer in Section-I, II, III of Paper-I; 0.50 marks in Paper-II and Paper-III. Section-III (CKT) and Section-IV (DEST) are qualifying in nature. Candidates must qualify all sections of Paper-I separately. Higher standards of CKT and DEST are set for specific posts (CSS, MEA, AFHQ, SFIO, CBIC, Postal etc.). Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour is available for PwBD candidates eligible for scribe.

SSC CGL 2026 – Scheme of Examination & Syllabus (Brief)

Paper / Section Key Topics
Tier-I: General Intelligence & Reasoning Analogies, similarities, space visualization, problem solving, arithmetic reasoning, number series, coding-decoding, Venn diagrams, embedded figures, critical thinking, emotional intelligence, social intelligence
Tier-I: General Awareness Current events, India and neighbouring countries, History, Culture, Geography, Economic Scene, General Policy, Scientific Research
Tier-I: Quantitative Aptitude Whole numbers, decimals, fractions, percentages, ratio, averages, SI/CI, profit & loss, discount, time & work, time & distance, algebra, geometry, mensuration, trigonometry, bar charts, pie charts
Tier-I: English Comprehension Grammar, vocabulary, comprehension, writing ability
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-I: Mathematical Abilities Number systems, arithmetic, algebra, geometry, mensuration, trigonometry, statistics & probability
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-II: English Language & Comprehension Vocabulary, grammar, synonyms, antonyms, idioms, sentence improvement, comprehension passages (current affairs + editorial based)
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-III: Computer Knowledge Computer basics, MS Office (Word/Excel/PowerPoint), Internet & e-mail, networking & cybersecurity basics
Tier-II Paper-II: Statistics Data collection, frequency distribution, central tendency, dispersion, correlation, regression, probability, sampling theory, statistical inference, ANOVA, time series, index numbers
Tier-II Paper-III: General Studies (Finance & Economics) Part A (Finance & Accounts, 80 marks): Financial accounting, GAAP, books of accounts, depreciation, bank reconciliation. Part B (Economics & Governance, 120 marks): Economic concepts, Indian economy, governance, financial markets, budget basics

SSC CGL 2026 – Exam Centres

The examination will be conducted through 9 SSC Regions covering all states and union territories. Candidates must choose three examination centres, in order of priority, within the same region.

Central Region: Agra, Lucknow, Prayagraj, Varanasi, Patna, Gaya Eastern Region: Kolkata, Ranchi, Bhubaneswar, Cuttack, Gangtok Karnataka Kerala Region: Bengaluru, Kochi, Kozhikode, Mysuru Madhya Pradesh Region: Bhopal, Indore, Jabalpur, Raipur North Eastern Region: Guwahati, Imphal, Shillong, Aizawl, Agartala Northern Region: Delhi, Jaipur, Dehradun North Western Region: Chandigarh, Shimla, Jammu, Srinagar, Amritsar Southern Region: Chennai, Hyderabad, Vijayawada, Coimbatore, Madurai Western Region: Mumbai, Pune, Ahmedabad, Surat, Nagpur, Panaji

No request for change of centre will be entertained after submission of the application. SSC may add, cancel, or shift candidates to another centre depending on administrative requirements.

Competition Level Analysis for SSC CGL 2026

The overall competition level for SSC CGL 2026 is expected to remain very high because the examination attracts a massive number of graduate applicants every year across India. Even with around 12,256 vacancies, the effective competition ratio remains extremely tough for high-demand posts.

  1. Applicant volume is huge: SSC CGL is among the most sought-after central government exams, and premium posts receive intense preference from top scorers.
  2. Tier-I acts as the main screening stage: With negative marking and sectional timing, both speed and accuracy matter. Candidates targeting top posts generally need a very strong Tier-I score.
  3. Tier-II gets post-specific: Specialized posts like JSO and AAO attract a smaller but more qualified pool, making domain preparation more important than raw competition numbers.
  4. DEST and CKT can eliminate candidates: Although qualifying in nature, poor performance in these sections can disqualify otherwise strong candidates for certain posts.
  5. Physical standards create an extra filter: Certain Inspector and Sub Inspector posts require physical and medical fitness standards that are checked separately by user departments.

Who Should NOT Apply for SSC CGL 2026?

  1. Candidates who will not possess a Bachelor's Degree by 01-08-2026.
  2. Candidates who fall outside the age limit for all eligible posts even after applicable relaxation.
  3. OBC Creamy Layer candidates intending to claim OBC reservation.
  4. Candidates who cannot meet post-specific physical or medical standards for the posts they plan to prefer.
  5. Non-matriculate Ex-Servicemen or those who have not completed the required length of service.
  6. Candidates applying for JSO without the required Mathematics or Statistics qualification.
  7. Female candidates applying specifically for BRO vacancies, where only male candidates are eligible.
  8. Distance-learning degree holders without valid UGC-DEB approval for the relevant period.

Common Mistakes / What to Check Before Applying SSC CGL 2026

  1. Not generating a fresh OTR on the new SSC website.
  2. Using a pre-existing photograph instead of live photo capture where required.
  3. Uploading blurred or wrongly sized signature files.
  4. Selecting the wrong reservation category without valid supporting certificate.
  5. Ignoring post-specific standards before locking post preferences.
  6. Choosing JSO or AAO related preferences without being ready for Paper-II or Paper-III.
  7. Skipping Aadhaar-based authentication even when eligible.
  8. Waiting until the final date for application submission.
  9. Missing the answer key objection window after examination.
  10. Confusing the correction window fee structure.

How to Apply for SSC CGL 2026

Candidates must apply online only at https://ssc.gov.in or through the mY SSC mobile application (available on Store). No other mode of application is accepted. Candidates who have not generated OTR on the new website must do so first. Once OTR is generated on the new website, it remains valid for all future examinations on that portal.

Candidates are advised to opt for Aadhaar-based authentication during OTR. Aadhaar-authenticated candidates get significant exemptions: their applications are not rejected for photograph/signature standards, and they need not carry photo ID at the examination venue.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Generate One-Time Registration (OTR) at https://ssc.gov.in (mandatory; use Aadhaar-based authentication if possible)
  2. Complete OTR profile and lock it
  3. Fill the Common Application Form (CAF) including live photograph capture via camera
  4. Upload scanned signature (JPEG/JPG, 10–20 KB, 6.0 cm × 2.0 cm)
  5. Fill the Examination Specific Module (post preferences, exam centre choices - 3 centres within same region in order of priority)
  6. Pay application fee of Rs. 100/- online (if applicable) by 23-06-2026
  7. Preview/Print the application to verify all details before final submission
  8. Use the correction window (29-06 to 01-07-2026) if any correction is needed (chargeable)
  9. Keep printout of application and all documents for at least 3 years

Documents to Keep Ready for Document Verification (after final selection):

  • Matriculation/Secondary School Certificate (proof of date of birth)
  • Mark sheets for all years/semesters of Graduation + Provisional/Degree Certificate
  • SC/ST Certificate in prescribed format (Annexure of the notification)
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format (Annexure-X); must be valid as on closing date of application
  • EWS Income & Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026–27 (issued based on income for FY 2025–26) in prescribed format (Annexure-XI)
  • PwBD Disability Certificate from competent authority specifying type of disability (as per Annexure-XII to XIII)
  • Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate and other relevant ESM documents
  • Equivalence certificate (for equivalent qualifications other than standard degrees)
  • DEB/UGC approval letter (for Distance Learning degree holders)
  • Certificate of proficiency in State Regional/official language (for AAO/Assistant Audit Officer State Cadre)

Apply Online at: https://ssc.gov.in | Toll Free Helpline: 1800 309 3063

Important Instructions for SSC CGL 2026 Candidates

  • Final allotment is based on merit-cum-preference, and no alternate post will be offered if a candidate fails post-specific standards after allotment.
  • OBC candidates claiming reservation must not belong to the creamy layer as on the closing date of application.
  • EWS certificate must be valid for FY 2026–27 and should be based on income for FY 2025–26.
  • PwBD candidates must choose the correct sub-category in the application form because no later change is allowed.
  • Scribe rules are strict: the scribe must complete OTR, be Aadhaar-authenticated, and cannot assist more than one candidate.
  • Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour is available to eligible PwBD candidates.
  • Tentative answer keys can be challenged online by paying Rs. 100 per question within the specified period only.
  • No re-evaluation or re-checking of scores is available at any stage.
  • Candidates should preserve the application form and related documents for at least 3 years.
  • Candidature remains provisional until certificates and documents are verified by the Appointing Authority.
  • Fraudulent claims of reservation status may lead to debarment from all SSC examinations.
  • Any update in exam schedule or process will be communicated through the SSC website only.
  • Applications without the required fee, unless exempted, or incomplete applications will be summarily rejected.

Check complete details for SBI 7150 Apprentice Online Form 2026. Includes important dates, application link, eligibility, syllabus and result info — updated live on OnlyJobs.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest State Bank of India (SBI) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Apprentice posts! Published on 19-05-2026 at the official website of SBI, this new recruitment drive offers 7150 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 20 to 28 years age group and possessing a Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute can apply online from 19-05-2026 to 08-06-2026. Find all details about the SBI Apprentice new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

State Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

State Bank of India, Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai has officially released Advertisement No. CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07 dated 19/05/2026 for engagement of 7150 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise and district-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official SBI website.

  • Download SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

State Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

SBI is recruiting 7150 Apprentice posts with Any Graduate qualification, last date 08 June 2026.

Organization Name State Bank of India (SBI), Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD), Mumbai
Advertisement No. CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07 dated 19/05/2026
Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Department Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD), State Bank of India
Total Training Seats 7150 (across 34 States/UTs, including PwBD seats)
Stipend Rs. 15,000/- per month (no other allowances/benefits)
Duration of Training 1 Year (On-the-Job Training)
Application Mode Online only (via NAPS portal + SBI website)
Selection Mode Online Written Test + Test of Local Language + Medical Examination
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in)
Online Examination Tentatively in July 2026
Official Website https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 19/05/2026
Opening of Online Registration of Application 19-05-2026
Last Date for Online Registration of Application 08-06-2026
Age Calculated As On 01-04-2026
Call Letter / Acquaint Yourself Booklet Download Tentatively in June 2026
Online Written Examination Date Tentatively in July 2026
Local Language Test Date To be notified after Online Written Examination result

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 7150 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only.

State / UT Language Total
Gujarat Gujarati 217
Andhra Pradesh Telugu / Urdu 709
Karnataka Kannada 276

Total   7150

Abbreviations: SC – Scheduled Caste, ST – Scheduled Tribe, OBC – Other Backward Classes, EWS – Economically Weaker Section, UR – Unreserved, VI – Visually Impaired, HI – Hearing Impaired, LD – Locomotor Disability, d&e – Autism Spectrum Disorder, Specific Learning Disorder, Mental Illness, and Multiple Disabilities. Reservation for PwBD is horizontal within overall vacancies. Change of category will not be permitted after registration of online application.

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) - Categories Eligible

Category Description
Visual Impairment (VI) Blindness (total absence of sight / visual acuity less than 3/60 in better eye / field less than 10°) and Low Vision (visual acuity not exceeding 6/18 / field less than 40°)
Hearing Impaired (HI) Hard of Hearing – 60 DB to 70 DB hearing loss in speech frequencies in both ears
Locomotor Disability (LD) OA, BA, OL, BL, OAL, Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Muscular Dystrophy, Acid Attack Victims, Spinal Deformity (SD) and Spinal Injury (SI) without associated neurological/limb dysfunction
d & e Categories Autism Spectrum Disorder (Mild); Multiple Disabilities among LD, VI, HI, SLD, MI & ASD

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the date of eligibility (01-04-2026). Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates must mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online through SBI website (https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers).
Educational Qualification (as on 01-04-2026) Graduation from a recognized University / Institute.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship either in SBI or in any other organization earlier, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training as per the Apprentices Act 1961 as amended.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after attainment of educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
CIBIL Candidates who have defaulted in repayment under any lending arrangement with Banks/NBFCs/Financial Institutions including credit card dues and have not regularized/repaid outstanding till date of issuance of offer of engagement shall not be eligible.
Aadhaar Requirement All candidates (except those belonging to Northeastern States where alternate ID proof will do) should produce Aadhaar Number before engagement, i.e., contract signing for apprenticeship training.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-04-2026, i.e., candidates must have been born not later than 01-04-2006 and not earlier than 02-04-1998 (both days inclusive). Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) / EWS 20 to 28 years (as on 01-04-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
PwBD As per Government of India guidelines

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. The process of registration is complete only when fee is deposited with the Bank through online mode on or before the last date for payment of fees. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

Category Fees / Intimation Charges
General / OBC / EWS Rs. 300/- (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD NIL

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on (i) Online Written Test and (ii) Test of Local Language, followed by Medical Examination. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility, qualifying in local language test, and medical fitness. A Wait List (State-category wise) will be maintained for one year from the date of result or next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier. Candidates will be released only once from this waitlist against non-joining subject to availability of qualified candidates.

Stage 1 - Online Written Examination (Objective Type)

Sl. Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
2 General English 25 25 15 minutes English only
3 Quantitative Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
4 Reasoning Ability & Computer Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
Total 100 100 60 minutes  

Each test has a separate timing as mentioned above. There will be NEGATIVE MARKS for wrong answers — 1/4th of the mark assigned for the question will be deducted for each wrong answer. Test questions (except General English) will be set in 13 Regional Languages viz. Assamese, Bengali, Gujarati, Kannada, Konkani, Malayalam, Manipuri, Marathi, Odia, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, Urdu — in addition to English and Hindi — as applicable State-wise. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order; tie-break is by age in descending order.

Medium of Examination – State/UT-wise

Sr. State/UT Medium of Exam Sr. State/UT Medium of Exam
1 Andaman & Nicobar English, Hindi 18 Maharashtra English, Hindi, Marathi
2 Andhra Pradesh English, Hindi, Telugu, Urdu 19 Manipur English, Hindi, Manipuri
3 Arunachal Pradesh English, Hindi 20 Meghalaya English, Hindi
4 Assam English, Hindi, Assamese, Bengali 21 Mizoram English, Hindi
5 Bihar English, Hindi 22 Nagaland English, Hindi
6 Chandigarh English, Hindi, Punjabi 23 Odisha English, Hindi, Odia
7 Chhattisgarh English, Hindi 24 Puducherry English, Hindi, Tamil
8 Goa English, Hindi, Konkani 25 Punjab English, Hindi, Punjabi
9 Gujarat English, Hindi, Gujarati 26 Rajasthan English, Hindi
10 Haryana English, Hindi, Punjabi 27 Sikkim English, Hindi
11 Himachal Pradesh English, Hindi 28 Tamil Nadu English, Hindi, Tamil
12 Jammu & Kashmir English, Hindi, Urdu 29 Telangana English, Hindi, Telugu, Urdu
13 Jharkhand English, Hindi 30 Tripura English, Hindi, Bengali
14 Karnataka English, Hindi, Kannada 31 Uttar Pradesh English, Hindi, Urdu
15 Kerala English, Hindi, Malayalam 32 Uttarakhand English, Hindi
16 Ladakh English, Hindi, Urdu 33 West Bengal English, Hindi, Bengali
17 Madhya Pradesh English, Hindi 34 Delhi English, Hindi

Stage 2 - Test of Local Language

The test of specified local language will be conducted after qualifying the online written examination. Candidates who fail to qualify this test will not be engaged as apprentice. Candidates who produce 10th or 12th standard mark sheet/certificate evidencing having studied the specified local language will not be required to undergo the language test. Candidates must appear at their own expense; SBI will not reimburse travelling expenses.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank and satisfactory completion of KYE formalities.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Weightage in Junior Associate Recruitment

Weightage/Relaxation to apprentices in recruitment of Junior Associates shall be given as per the recruitment policy of the Bank as amended from time to time, to apprentices who successfully complete the apprenticeship training in SBI, subject to candidate's satisfactory conduct and performance during the entire training period. Successful completion refers to passing of the skill evaluation test (Trade test) and obtention of the joint National Apprenticeship Certificate issued by SBI-NSDC / BFSI-SSC.

Competition Level Analysis for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The competition level for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be very high due to 7150 seats being offered at the all-India level and the exam being open to any graduate, including those already preparing for bank and SSC exams. While the written test is of easy to moderate difficulty and slightly below SBI Clerk level, the large number of serious aspirants in high-population states like Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, West Bengal, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana will push state-wise and category-wise cut-offs upward.

In practical terms, each seat is likely to attract dozens to over a hundred candidates in major states, making speed, accuracy, and sectional balance crucial to clearing the single-stage written exam followed by the local language test. However, the effective competition reduces at later stages because some candidates will fail to qualify the language test or be filtered out at the document verification, CIBIL, Aadhaar, and medical fitness stages, and SBI will finally select candidates strictly on state-wise and category-wise merit from those who clear all stages.

Who should NOT apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent job in SBI; this is only a 1‑year apprenticeship with no guarantee of absorption or regular employment.
  • Candidates who already completed any Apprenticeship (in SBI or any other organisation) or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act.
  • Candidates outside the age band 20–28 years as on the cut-off date (even 1 day younger/older is not eligible, except notified relaxations for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD).
  • Aspirants who do not have a Graduation degree from a recognised University/Institute by the specified date.
  • Candidates not fluent in the notified local language of the State/UT (reading, writing, speaking, understanding), and who are unlikely to clear the language test or do not have 10th/12th proof.
  • Candidates unwilling or unable to register on the NAPS portal before applying, as this is a mandatory prerequisite for SBI Apprentice.
  • Candidates with serious CIBIL/loan/credit‑card defaults who have not regularised or repaid outstanding dues by the time of offer; such profiles are normally rejected in bank recruitments.
  • Ex‑Servicemen / Disabled Ex‑Servicemen, where the notification clearly states no engagement / no reservation for them under Apprentice.
  • PwBD candidates whose disability type falls under “likely to improve” (temporary disability) where reservation benefits may not apply as per notification conditions.
  • Candidates who cannot produce valid Aadhaar (except where exempted, e.g., certain North‑Eastern states with alternate ID allowed).

Common mistakes / What to check before applying SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Applying in a wrong State or language where you cannot comfortably read, write and speak the specified local language.
  • Entering wrong category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR); category cannot be changed later and mismatch with certificates at DV can lead to cancellation.
  • OBC candidates from creamy layer applying as OBC instead of UR; only non‑creamy OBCs with valid, recent certificate get reservation.
  • Uploading blurred/incorrect photo, signature, thumb impression or declaration (wrong size, wrong format, someone else’s handwriting) leading to rejection at scrutiny.
  • Using invalid or inactive mobile number/email ID, causing failure to receive OTPs, registration details, call letters or further communication.
  • Submitting multiple applications for different states or with different details; SBI usually accepts only the last complete application and may forfeit extra fees.
  • Ignoring negative marking and over‑attempting in the exam, which can sharply reduce your score despite high attempts.
  • Not reading the clause that this is not regular employment, and later protesting about absorption or permanent posting after apprenticeship ends.

How to Apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online; no other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and read the detailed guidelines on https://bfsissc.com before applying online through the SBI website.

Candidates should have a valid email ID and mobile number which should be kept active till declaration of results. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date and not wait till the last date.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded (as per Annexure-I):

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 200×230 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG)
  • Signature with black ink on white paper (140×60 pixels; 10 KB–20 KB; JPEG)
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink (240×240 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG/JPG)
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink (800×400 pixels; 50 KB–100 KB; JPEG/JPG) — in candidate's own handwriting
  • Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at the time of online application)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Examination / Document Verification / Engagement:

  • Call Letter (downloaded from Bank's website) along with photo identity proof (Passport / Aadhaar / PAN Card / Driving License / Voter's Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Identity Card issued by School or College / Gazetted Officer in official letterhead) in original and self-attested photocopy
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST candidates) — to be submitted at the time of engagement if called for
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed Government of India format, issued during the period 01-04-2026 to the date of joining (for OBC candidates)
  • Income and Asset Certificate issued by Competent Authority in the format prescribed by Government of India, valid for the year 2026-27 (for EWS candidates)
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by a Competent Authority as per Government of India guidelines (for PwBD candidates)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marksheets (Graduation onwards)

Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to SBI. No Hard Copy of the Call Letter for Online Written Examination will be sent by post. Candidates must appear for the test at their own cost; SBI will not pay or reimburse any such expenses.

Apply Online at: https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers

For queries: Call 022-22820427 (11:00 AM to 06:00 PM on working days) or visit https://cgrs.ibps.in — mention "Engagement of Apprentice in SBI" in the subject.

Important Instructions for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. NOR IS THE SAME CONTRACT EMPLOYMENT. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of SBI and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for employees of the Bank.
  • SBI shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to Apprentices during and/or after the completion of the Apprenticeship period. After completion, candidates will be relieved from the respective work area.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.
  • Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online on SBI website is required.
  • No Ex-Servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an Apprentice and there is no reservation for Ex-Servicemen in the Apprentice Engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General or General (LD/VI/HI/d&e) as applicable.
  • Change of category will not be permitted after registration of online application.
  • Candidates with CIBIL default record (unpaid loans/credit card dues) are not eligible for engagement until dues are regularized/repaid.
  • All candidates (except Northeastern State candidates) must produce Aadhaar Number before engagement/contract signing.
  • Not more than one application should be submitted; in case of multiple applications, only the last valid completed application will be retained and fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • PwBD candidates with disability conditions falling under "likely to improve" (temporary disability) category are not eligible for reservation.
  • PwBD candidates eligible for scribe facility: candidates with 40% or more disability having physical limitation to write are eligible. Scribe must be arranged by the candidate at his/her own cost. Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour of examination is allowed (whether availing scribe or not) for eligible PwBD candidates.
  • Candidates are advised to keep their registered email ID/mobile active for receiving call letters/advices etc.
  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, pagers, earphones or any other electronic/communication devices are not allowed inside the examination premises.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only — candidates should visit regularly.
  • No request for change of examination centre shall be entertained. No Travel Expenses will be reimbursed/paid by SBI.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.
  • Bank reserves right to cancel the selection process partly/entirely at any stage.

Key Highlights

  • Vacancy: C-DAC is recruiting for 900+ contractual positions across 11 centres including Bengaluru, Chennai, Noida, Pune, Mumbai, Hyderabad, Delhi, Mohali, Kolkata, Thiruvananthapuram, and Guwahati.
  • Qualification: BE/B.Tech, ME/M.Tech, MCA, M.Sc., Ph.D., ITI, Diploma, Graduation, MBA, CA, CMA, CS, LLB, and other relevant qualifications are eligible as per post-wise requirements.
  • Important Dates: Online applications start on 01 June 2026 and close on 20 June 2026.
  • Salary: Monthly remuneration ranges from ₹20,200 to ₹1,60,000 depending on the post and experience.
  • Age Limit: Upper age limit ranges from 30 to 56 years depending on the position. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India rules.
  • Apply Mode: Online only through the C-DAC Careers Portal.
  • Application Fee: No application fee for any category.
  • Selection: Initial screening based on qualifications and experience, followed by Written Test (if applicable) and Interview for shortlisted candidates.
  • Job Location: Across India

The latest C-DAC Recruitment 2026 has been announced for various contractual positions under the JIT (Just-In-Time) June 2026 Cycle! Published on 01-06-2026 at the official C-DAC careers portal, this massive pan-India recruitment drive covers multiple centres including Bengaluru, Chennai, Noida, Pune, Mumbai, Hyderabad, Delhi, Mohali, Kolkata, Thiruvananthapuram, and the North East (Guwahati/CINE). Eligible candidates with BE/B.Tech, ME/M.Tech, MCA, Ph.D., or relevant graduation/post-graduation in ICT&E domains can apply online from 01-06-2026 to 20-06-2026. Find all details about C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment including eligibility, vacancies, salary, and notification PDFs here.

FOLLOW US

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment 2026 Notification PDFs

Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC), a Scientific Society under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, has officially released separate notifications dated 01/06/2026 for engagement of contractual staff across its centres under the JIT June 2026 Recruitment Cycle (Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026). Each centre has issued its own advertisement covering position details, domain areas, qualification, experience, age limit, and application instructions. No application fee is charged for any of these advertisements. The last date to apply online is 20 June 2026 across all centres.

  • C-DAC Bengaluru Official Notification – Advt. No. C-DAC/BLR/02/2026
  • C-DAC Chennai Official Notification – Advt. No. C-DAC/CH/05/2026/8010
  • C-DAC CINE (Guwahati / North East) Official Notification – Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026-CINE
  • C-DAC Thiruvananthapuram Official Notification – Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026-TVM
  • C-DAC Pune Official Notification – Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026-PN
  • C-DAC Noida Official Notification – Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026-ND
  • C-DAC Mumbai Official Notification – Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026
  • C-DAC Mohali Official Notification – Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026-ML
  • C-DAC Kolkata Official Notification – Advt. No. C-DAC/KL/05/2026/4265
  • C-DAC Hyderabad Official Notification – Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026-HY
  • C-DAC Delhi Official Notification – Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026-DL

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment Overview

C-DAC is recruiting across 11 centres pan-India for contractual positions including Project Associate, Project Engineer, Senior Project Engineer, Project Manager, and support roles in domains spanning AI/ML, Cyber Security, VLSI, HPC, Embedded Systems, Full Stack Development, Quantum Computing, and more. No application fee is charged, and the last date to apply online is 20 June 2026.

Organization Name Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC), A Scientific Society under MeitY, Government of India
Recruitment Cycle JIT (Just-In-Time) June 2026 Cycle – Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026 (centre-wise suffixes)
Post Names Project Associate, Project Technician, Project Support Staff, Project Engineer / PS&O Executive, Senior Project Engineer / Module Lead / Project Leader, Project Officer, Project Manager / Program Manager / Program Delivery Manager / Knowledge Partner, Project Head, Administrative Associate
Nature of Appointment Purely contractual on consolidated emoluments – initially up to 3 years or project completion (whichever is earlier); extendable based on performance and project requirement. No right to regular/permanent appointment in C-DAC.
Centres / Locations Bengaluru, Chennai, Noida, Pune, Mumbai, Hyderabad, Delhi, Mohali, Kolkata, Thiruvananthapuram, Guwahati (CINE/North East) – and project sites across India
Salary Range Project Associate: Rs. 28,750 – Rs. 35,000/month | Project Engineer: Rs. 39,100 – Rs. 50,000/month | Senior Project Engineer: Rs. 60,000 – Rs. 75,000/month | Project Manager: Rs. 64,000 – Rs. 1,60,000/month (varies by centre and domain)
Application Fee NIL – No application fee is charged by C-DAC for any of these advertisements
Application Mode Online only – via C-DAC Careers Portal (https://careers.cdac.in/)
Selection Mode Initial screening based on academic records → Written Test (if applicable) → Interview (shortlisted candidates only; intimated by email)
Official Website https://www.cdac.in/ | https://careers.cdac.in/

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment – Important Dates

The application window is open from 01 June 2026 and closes on 20 June 2026 across all centres. The interview/written test date will be communicated later by email only to shortlisted candidates.

Event Date
Commencement of Online Registration of Application 01 June 2026
Last Date for Online Registration of Application 20 June 2026 (time varies by centre: 18:00 / 23:55 / 23:59 hrs)
Age and Experience Cut-off Date Last Date of Submission of Application (i.e., 20 June 2026)
Written Test / Interview Date Will be communicated later by email only

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment – Centre-wise Vacancy Details

Vacancies are spread across 11 C-DAC centres. The number of positions indicated is provisional and may vary based on actual project requirements. Candidates must apply separately for each post they wish to be considered for.

Sr. No. C-DAC Centre Advt. No. Key Domains Approx. Vacancies

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment – Position-wise Details (All Centres Combined)

The table below gives the standardised position levels, their qualification requirements, experience bands, salary ranges, and upper age limits as applicable across C-DAC centres. Individual centres may have slight variations; always refer to the specific centre notification for exact position requirements.

Position Level Educational Qualification Experience Upper Age Limit Consolidated Salary (per month)

Project Manager / Program Manager / Program Delivery Manager / Knowledge Partner BE/B.Tech with 60% or equivalent CGPA OR PG in Science/Computer Application/relevant domain with 60% CGPA OR ME/M.Tech OR Ph.D. in relevant discipline Min 9–15 years 56 Years Rs. 64,000 – Rs. 1,60,000

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment – Eligibility Criteria

All candidates must hold qualifications from AICTE/UGC-approved colleges or recognized universities. Candidates in their final semester may apply but can join only after clearing the examination with the required minimum marks.

Criteria Details
Nationality Citizen of India (standard Government of India norms apply)
Educational Qualification BE/B.Tech or equivalent degree (min 60% or equivalent CGPA for most posts; 50% for Project Associate at some centres) OR PG in Science/Computer Application/relevant domain with requisite percentage OR ME/M.Tech OR Ph.D. in relevant discipline – from AICTE/UGC-approved institution or recognized university. For support/admin posts: Graduation with 50% + relevant experience.
CGPA/Grade Proof Candidates from universities using CGPA/DGPA/OGPA or letter grade systems must submit percentage conversion proof issued by the university along with the degree certificate.
Final Semester Candidates May apply; if selected, joining is permitted only after passing the final exam with the required minimum marks.
Experience Post-qualification work experience as specified against each post. Academic/teaching/research/internship/project placements as part of curriculum are NOT counted as experience unless specifically mentioned. Experience must be supported by certificates from employer clearly showing period, salary, and post held.
C-DAC Internal Candidates Must have completed at least 2 years of service in C-DAC as on the date of notification to be eligible to apply.
Nature of Engagement Purely contractual – no right to permanent/regular appointment. Contract is initially up to 3 years or project completion, whichever is earlier. Selected candidates may be deputed/transferred to any project location across India.

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment – Age Limit

Upper age limits vary by position level and are calculated as on the last date of application, i.e., 20 June 2026. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen and Government employees are eligible for age relaxation as per Government of India norms.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
Project Associate / Project Engineer (Fresher) 30 Years
Project Engineer (Experienced) / PS&O Executive 45 Years
Senior Project Engineer / Module Lead / Project Leader 40 Years
Project Support Staff / HRD / Group Secretary 35 Years
Project Technician 30 Years
Project Officer 50 Years
Project Manager / Program Manager / Program Delivery Manager / Knowledge Partner 56 Years
SC / ST 5 years relaxation over prescribed upper age limit
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation over prescribed upper age limit
PwBD / Physically Challenged / Ex-Servicemen As per Government of India norms
Government Employees (on pay scale) 5 years relaxation (in addition to other applicable relaxations)

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment – Application Fee

C-DAC charges absolutely no application fee for any position under this recruitment cycle - this applies to all categories including General, SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and PwBD candidates across all 11 centres.

Category Application Fee
All Categories (General / EWS / OBC / SC / ST / PwBD) NIL – No fee charged

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment – Selection Process

Selection is multi-stage: initial academic screening, followed by a written test if required, and a personal interview. Only shortlisted candidates are contacted by email - no hard copy communication is sent at any stage.

Stage 1 – Initial Screening

All applications are screened based on academic records, qualifications, experience, and other parameters declared in the online application. Only candidates who meet the minimum eligibility criteria are screened in for further selection. C-DAC reserves the right to raise the minimum cut-off if the number of applicants is significantly higher than vacancies.

Stage 2 – Written Test (if applicable)

Depending on the number of screened-in applicants and centre requirements, a written/skill test may be conducted. This is at the discretion of the respective C-DAC centre. Candidates will be informed through email only. No TA/DA is paid for attending the written test.

Stage 3 – Personal Interview

Shortlisted candidates are called for a personal interview by the respective C-DAC centre. The interview call letter will be sent via email only. Mere receipt of an interview call letter does not confirm selection or acceptance of candidature. No TA/DA is paid for attending the interview.

C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment – Centre-wise Contact and Jurisdiction

Each C-DAC centre manages its own selection process. Candidates must address queries only to the specific centre they have applied to. All disputes are subject to the courts/tribunals of the respective centre's city.

Centre HR / Recruitment Email Jurisdiction

Chennai [email protected] Chennai, Tamil Nadu
CINE / Guwahati (North East) [email protected] / [email protected] Guwahati, Assam
Thiruvananthapuram [email protected] Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala
Pune [email protected] Pune, Maharashtra
Noida [email protected] Noida / New Delhi
Mumbai [email protected] Mumbai, Maharashtra
Mohali [email protected] Mohali, Punjab
Kolkata [email protected] Delhi
Hyderabad [email protected] Hyderabad, Telangana
Delhi [email protected] Delhi

Competition Level Analysis for C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment

The C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment is a large-scale opportunity with openings across 11 centres and hundreds of positions spanning fresher to senior levels. Competition is expected to be moderate to high for roles in high-demand domains such as AI/ML, Full Stack Development, Cyber Security, and Software Development, particularly at the Project Engineer and Senior Project Engineer levels, which attract the largest pools of applicants from tier-2 and tier-3 engineering colleges. Vacancies for niche domains like Quantum Computing, VLSI, Additive Manufacturing, Robotics, and HPC System Software will see comparatively lower competition but require highly specialised credentials. The absence of any application fee significantly increases the applicant volume.

Effective competition will be filtered through the initial academic screening stage, where CGPA, percentage, and domain relevance determine shortlisting. Candidates who have internships, publications, or project work in their declared domain may gain an advantage even if such experience is not counted formally. For experienced positions, those with documented employer-issued experience certificates closely matching the domain will clear the first cut. Candidates applying to Noida (e-Governance, Health Informatics) and Pune (high volume, 200+ positions) will face steeper competition due to location preference and breadth of domain coverage.

Who Should NOT Apply for C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent government job - all positions are purely contractual with no guarantee of regular appointment in C-DAC.
  • Candidates whose qualification is from a college/university NOT approved by AICTE/UGC or not recognized by the Central/State Government - their candidature will be rejected.
  • Candidates unable to produce CGPA-to-percentage conversion proof from their university if their institution uses a CGPA/letter grade system.
  • Candidates who want to count internship, project placements, teaching, or research work as experience - these are explicitly excluded unless the specific post mentions otherwise.
  • Candidates without valid experience certificates from past/present employers clearly showing period of employment, salary, and designation - experience without documentary proof will not be considered.
  • C-DAC internal candidates who have not completed at least 2 years of service in C-DAC as on 20 June 2026.
  • Candidates unwilling to be deputed or transferred to any C-DAC project location across India - selected candidates may be posted anywhere in India as per organizational requirement.
  • Candidates who are not comfortable with purely email-based communication - all call letters, interview invitations, and correspondence are sent by email only; no hard copies are sent.
  • Candidates whose age exceeds the upper age limit for the applied post as on 20 June 2026, and who do not possess valid category certificates for applicable relaxations.
  • Candidates seeking to canvass or influence the selection process - canvassing in any form is a disqualification.

Common Mistakes / What to Check Before Applying for C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment?

  • Applying to the wrong centre - each centre has a separate advertisement and separate application link; applying on one centre's form does not cover other centres.
  • Not submitting a separate application for each post - candidates applying for multiple positions must submit individual applications for each one.
  • Uploading a resume in the wrong format - only PDF format (max 500 KB) is accepted; photographs must be in .jpg format (max 400 KB).
  • Not noting the unique application number generated after submission - this is needed for future reference and tracking.
  • Government/PSU/Autonomous body employees forgetting to forward their signed application through proper channel to the respective HRD section - those not doing so must produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their current employer at the time of interview.
  • Claiming experience from internships, academic projects, or teaching without checking whether the specific post allows it - most posts do not count such work as experience.
  • Sending hard copies of applications, mark sheets, or resumes to C-DAC - no documents should be sent physically; all applications are online only.
  • Assuming the interview call letter confirms selection - it does not; candidature is subject to document verification at any stage.
  • Internal C-DAC candidates forgetting that if finally selected, they must resign from their current C-DAC service and join as a fresh employee for the selected post.
  • Not visiting the official C-DAC website regularly - all corrigenda, extensions, and updates are published only on www.cdac.in and no other platform.

How to Apply for C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment

Candidates must apply online only through the C-DAC careers portal (https://careers.cdac.in/). No offline/hard copy applications are accepted. A valid mobile number and email ID are mandatory as OTP-based login is used and all communications are sent by email only.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:

  • Passport-size photograph in .jpg format (not more than 400 KB)
  • Resume / CV in PDF format (not more than 500 KB)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Joining:

  • All educational mark sheets and degree certificates from Class 10 onwards
  • CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate issued by the university (if applicable)
  • Experience certificates from all previous and current employers clearly indicating period of employment, salary drawn, and post held
  • Caste/category certificate in prescribed Government of India format (SC/ST/OBC Non-Creamy Layer/EWS) where applicable
  • Disability certificate in prescribed format issued by the District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
  • SSLC/SSC/ISC mark sheet or other valid document as proof of date of birth
  • No Objection Certificate (NOC) from current employer (if not forwarded through proper channel)
  • Any other document evidencing eligibility as declared in the application

Apply Online at: https://careers.cdac.in/ → Select the relevant centre → Click 'Apply' against the desired position → Log in with mobile number → Enter OTP → Fill and submit application form.

Important Instructions for C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment Candidates

  • These are CONTRACTUAL positions - no right to regular or permanent employment in C-DAC is created by engagement under this recruitment.
  • Candidates may be deputed or transferred from one project to another at any C-DAC project location across India based on organizational requirement.
  • The contract is initially for up to 3 years (1 year for Kolkata centre) or project completion, whichever is earlier. Extension is possible based on performance and project requirement.
  • All correspondence including interview call letters will be sent by email only - candidates must keep their registered email ID and mobile number active throughout the process.
  • C-DAC reserves the right to increase/decrease the number of posts, change eligibility criteria, cancel any or all positions, or modify/cancel the selection process at any stage without notice.
  • If selected candidates are found ineligible at any stage - even after joining - their engagement is liable to be terminated without notice.
  • No TA/DA is payable for attending the written test or interview at any centre.
  • C-DAC has not authorized any agent or agency to represent it for recruitment matters - beware of fraudulent offers.
  • All updates, corrigenda, and extensions will be published only on the official website www.cdac.in.
  • Canvassing in any form will result in disqualification.

The latest Tamilnad Mercantile Bank (TMB) Recruitment 2026 has been officially announced for Agricultural Officer positions in the Assistant Manager cadre. Released in May 2026 on the bank's career portal, this prestigious recruitment drive by one of South India's leading old-generation private sector banks offers excellent career opportunities for agriculture graduates with relevant banking experience. The online application window is open from 22 May 2026 to 06 June 2026. This is a regular employment opportunity with posting anywhere in India. Find complete details about TMB Agricultural Officer Vacancy 2026, eligibility criteria, selection process, roles & responsibilities, salary, application procedure, and official notification PDF below.

FOLLOW US

Tamilnad Mercantile Bank Agricultural Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Tamilnad Mercantile Bank Ltd., headquartered in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu, has issued the official Recruitment Advertisement for the post of Agricultural Officer. The detailed notification PDF contains exhaustive information regarding educational qualifications, experience requirements, age limit, roles and responsibilities, selection methodology, general conditions, and step-by-step application guidelines. All aspiring candidates are strongly advised to download and thoroughly read the full notification before proceeding with the online application to avoid any disqualification at later stages.

  • Download TMB Agricultural Officer Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

Tamilnad Mercantile Bank (TMB) is inviting applications from dynamic, result-oriented, and agriculture-domain professionals for engagement as Agricultural Officer on a regular basis. This is a specialist officer recruitment aimed at strengthening the bank's rural and agricultural lending portfolio across India.

TMB Agricultural Officer Vacancy Details 2026 

Although the exact number of vacancies has not been mentioned in the advertisement, TMB is expected to recruit multiple Agricultural Officers depending upon the business requirements of the bank. Selected candidates will be posted in rural and semi-urban branches across India where agricultural lending is a key focus area. The bank reserves the right to increase or decrease the number of posts without prior notice.

Post Name Cadre Total Vacancies
Agricultural Officer Assistant Manager (Scale-I) Not Mentioned

TMB Agricultural Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must satisfy all the eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 30.04.2026. Only Indian citizens are eligible to apply.

Criteria Details
Nationality Indian Citizen only
Educational Qualification Graduation / Degree in Agriculture or allied subjects from a recognized University with minimum 50% aggregate marks. Preference will be given to candidates having specializations in Horticulture, Animal Husbandry, Veterinary Science, Dairy Science, or Agricultural Engineering.
Experience Minimum 3 years of post-qualification relevant experience in agricultural lending / rural banking in Scheduled Commercial Banks, Regional Rural Banks (RRBs), NABARD, or any other financial institutions.
Prior Employment Candidates currently employed in other banks/financial institutions must submit proper relieving letter and experience certificates.
Computer Knowledge Working knowledge of MS Office and core banking software will be an added advantage.

 TMB Agricultural Officer Age Limit 2026

Category Age Limit

No application fee is required. The registration process is free of cost for all candidates.
