📄 Complete Details & Notification

Key Highlights

  • Vacancy: Indian Navy is recruiting Agniveer (Apprentice) - 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batch from unmarried male candidates across Engineering and Electrical streams.
  • Qualification: Candidates must have passed Matriculation with minimum 50% marks and completed a 3-year Diploma from an AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute with minimum 50% marks in the relevant trade.
  • Important Dates: Online applications open from 05 June 2026 and close on 29 June 2026 (17:00 hrs). Correction window closes 06 July 2026.
  • Salary: Starting package of ₹30,000 per month (In-Hand 70% = ₹21,000) in Year 1, increasing to ₹40,000 per month by Year 4. Seva Nidhi corpus of ₹10.04 Lakhs on completion of 4-year tenure.
  • Selection: Stage I - INET (Indian Navy Entrance Test) → Stage II - Physical Fitness Test (PFT) + Written Examination + Recruitment Medical Examination → Pre-Enrolment Medical at INS Chilka.
  • Age Limit: 01/2027 Batch: Born between 01 Dec 2004 - 31 May 2009. 02/2027 Batch: Born between 01 May 2005 - 31 Oct 2009 (both dates inclusive).
  • Apply Mode: Online only through https://joinindiannavy.gov.in
  • Job Location: All India (Transferable as per Indian Navy requirements)

The latest Indian Navy Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Agniveer (Apprentice) posts! Published through the Directorate of Manpower Planning & Recruitment, Naval Headquarters, New Delhi on the official website joinindiannavy.gov.in, this new recruitment drive offers vacancies to be decided based on service requirements for eligible candidates. Unmarried Indian male candidates possessing an AICTE-recognised Diploma in the relevant Engineering or Electrical discipline can apply online from 05 June 2026 to 29 June 2026. Find all details about the Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Directorate of Manpower Planning & Recruitment, Naval Headquarters, New Delhi has officially released the advertisement for Agniveer (Apprentice) - 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batch under the Agnipath Scheme. The notification covers complete details on eligibility, age criteria, pay package, Seva Nidhi, selection process, medical standards, and general instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official Indian Navy website.

  • Download Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Indian Navy - Directorate of Manpower Planning & Recruitment, Naval Headquarters, New Delhi
Advertisement Agniveer (Apprentice) - 01/2027 and 02/2027 Batch
Post Name Agniveer (Apprentice)
Streams / Trades 1. Engineering - Marine Engineering & Systems, Mechanical, Production/Automobile, Refrigeration & AC, Power Plant, Mechatronics, Metallurgy, Instrumentation, Electronics Instrumentation & Control Engineering   2. Electrical - Electrical, Electronics & Communication, Instrumentation Technology, Electronics & Control Engineering
Total Vacancies To be decided based on service requirement
Pay Package ₹30,000/month (Year 1) to ₹40,000/month (Year 4); Seva Nidhi corpus ₹10.04 Lakhs on completion
Job Location All India (Transferable; posting at any Indian Navy location, JV or subsidiary)
Application Mode Online only (via https://joinindiannavy.gov.in)
Selection Mode Stage I - INET → Stage II - PFT + Written Exam + Recruitment Medical → Pre-Enrolment Medical at INS Chilka
Duration of Service 4 Years under the Agnipath Scheme (Navy Act 1957)
Official Website https://joinindiannavy.gov.in

Also Read: BSNL 100 JTO Recruitment 2026 - Apply Now 

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Tentative Date
Application Window Opens (Stage I - INET 2/2026) for 01/2027 & 02/2027 Batch 05 Jun 2026
Application Window Closes 29 Jun 2026 (17:00 hrs)
Correction Window 06 Jul 2026
Stage I - INET Examination Aug 2026
Result Declaration of Stage I - INET End Aug / Early Sep 2026
Shortlisting & Issue of Call-up Letters - 01/2027 Batch Sep 2026
Stage II for Agniveer (Apprentice) 01/2027 Oct 2026
Induction at INS Chilka for 01/2027 Batch Dec 2026
Shortlisting & Issue of Call-up Letters - 02/2027 Batch Feb 2027
Stage II for Agniveer (Apprentice) 02/2027 Mar 2027
Induction at INS Chilka for 02/2027 Batch May 2027

*All dates are tentative. Exact dates of each activity will be promulgated on the Candidates Registration Portal at https://joinindiannavy.gov.in.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

Vacancies for Agniveer (Apprentice) 01/2027 and 02/2027 batches are distributed across Engineering and Electrical streams. The exact number of trade-wise vacancies will be decided based on service requirement. Separate INET examinations will be conducted for Electrical and Engineering entries.

Stream Trade / Diploma Discipline Vacancies
Engineering Marine Engineering & Systems, Mechanical To be notified based on service requirement
Production / Automobile
Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Repair and Maintenance
Power Plant Engineering
Machine, Manufacturing / Manufacturing Technology
Mechatronics
Metallurgy
Instrumentation, Electronics Instrumentation and Control Engineering
Industrial/Production, Maintenance
Electrical Electrical, Electronics & Communication To be notified based on service requirement
Instrumentation Technology
Electronics and Control Engineering
Electrical and Electronics

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Post-wise Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfil all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date. Only unmarried Indian male candidates are eligible to apply. All qualifications must be from AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institutes.

Agniveer (Apprentice) - Engineering & Electrical Streams

Criteria Details
Essential Educational Qualification Passed Matriculation Examination with minimum 50% marks in aggregate from Boards of School Education recognised by Ministry of Education, Govt. of India AND completed a 3-year Diploma course from an AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute with minimum 50% marks in aggregate in any of the qualifying diploma trades (Engineering or Electrical as applicable).
Marital Status Only unmarried male candidates are eligible. Candidates must give a certificate of being 'unmarried' at the time of enrolment. Agniveers will not be permitted to marry during their entire 4-year tenure. A candidate will be dismissed from service if he marries during tenure or is found to be already married despite giving an unmarried certificate.
Nationality Indian male citizens only.
Preferential Proficiency in sports, swimming, and extra-curricular activities is desirable.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Batch Date of Birth (Both Dates Inclusive)
Agniveer (Apprentice) 01/2027 Batch 01 December 2004 - 31 May 2009
Agniveer (Apprentice) 02/2027 Batch 01 May 2005 - 31 October 2009
Eligible for INET 2/2026 Candidates born between 01 Dec 2004 - 31 Oct 2009 are eligible to appear in INET 2/2026. They will be shortlisted for Stage II of the respective batch based on the batch-wise age criteria above.

Note: There are no age relaxations for Ex-Servicemen status for Agniveer enrolment.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Category Application Fee
All Candidates (Stage I - INET) ₹550/- + 18% GST (Total as applicable); payable online only
Stage II Process No separate fee for Stage II
Payment Mode Online only - Net Banking / Visa / Master / RuPay Credit Card / Debit Card / UPI
Sponsor Candidates & NCC 'C' Certificate Holders Exempted from Stage I - INET; directly issued call-up for Stage II based on eligibility

Note: Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstances. Candidates found to have registered multiple times will be rejected and debarred; fee reimbursement will not be carried out in such cases. In case of unsuccessful payment (money deducted but admit card not generated), wait 7 working days for automatic refund.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Stage 1 - INET (Indian Navy Entrance Test)

Separate INET exams will be conducted for Electrical and Engineering entries. INET 2/2026 will be conducted in August 2026 with SSC as Exam Partner.

Parameter Details
Mode Computer-based
Total Questions 100 questions of 1 mark each
Language Bilingual - Hindi & English
Type Objective / Multiple Choice
Sections English & General Awareness, Science, Mathematics, and Diploma (Trade Specific)
Duration 1 Hour
Negative Marking 0.25 marks deducted per wrong answer
Passing Criteria Must pass in all sections as well as in aggregate. Indian Navy reserves the right to determine pass marks in each section and in aggregate.
Centre Allocation At the discretion of the Indian Navy; state preference to be indicated by candidate

Stage 2 - Physical Fitness Test (PFT)

Qualifying in PFT is mandatory for selection. Candidates undergoing PFT do so at their own risk.

Gender 1.6 Km Run Squats (Uthak Baithak) Push-ups Bent Knee Sit-ups
Male 06 min 30 sec 20 15 15

Stage 2 - Written Examination

Written exam is conducted only for candidates who qualify PFT. Two separate exams are conducted for Engineering and Electrical streams.

Parameter Details
Total Questions 100 questions of 1 mark each
Language Bilingual - Hindi & English
Type Objective / Multiple Choice
Sections English & General Awareness, Science, Mathematics, and Diploma (Trade Specific)
Duration 1 Hour
Passing Criteria Must pass in all sections as well as in aggregate

Stage 2 - Recruitment Medical Examination

Conducted for all candidates who qualify PFT. Candidates found medically unfit may appeal within 05 days at a designated Military Hospital allocated by the Indian Navy. Medical fitness certificates from hospitals other than designated Military Hospitals will not be considered. No second appeal is permissible.

Stage 3 - Pre-Enrolment Medical at INS Chilka

Pre-enrolment medical formalities of all selected candidates will be conducted at INS Chilka, Odisha. Induction is subject to qualifying in Pre-Enrolment Medical Examination. Candidates not qualifying will be rejected.

Also Read: SSB Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 404 Constable GD Posts

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Emoluments / Pay Package

Year Customised Package (Monthly) In Hand - 70% (Monthly) Contribution to Agniveer Corpus Fund - 30% (Monthly) Contribution to Corpus Fund by GoI (Monthly)
1st Year ₹30,000 ₹21,000 ₹9,000 ₹9,000
2nd Year ₹33,000 ₹23,100 ₹9,900 ₹9,900
3rd Year ₹36,500 ₹25,550 ₹10,950 ₹10,950
4th Year ₹40,000 ₹28,000 ₹12,000 ₹12,000
Total in Agniveer Corpus Fund (Seva Nidhi) ₹5.02 Lakh ₹5.02 Lakh

Total Seva Nidhi on completion of 4-year tenure: ₹10.04 Lakhs (Agniveer's contribution + matching GoI contribution). Additional benefits include:

  • Risk, hardship, dress and travel allowances
  • Non-contributory Life Insurance Cover of ₹48 Lakh for the duration of engagement
  • Death Compensation: One-time ex-gratia of ₹44 Lakh (attributable to service) in addition to insurance cover
  • Disability Compensation: One-time ex-gratia of ₹44 / ₹25 / ₹15 Lakh based on 100% / 75% / 50% disability
  • 30 days leave per year; sick leave as per medical advice
  • Medical facilities at service hospitals and CSD provisions
  • No gratuity or pensionary benefits applicable

How to Apply for Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026

Candidates must apply online only at https://joinindiannavy.gov.in. No other mode of application will be accepted. Applications can also be uploaded through Common Service Centres (CSC) across the country at a fee of ₹60 + GST (optional facility).

Steps to Apply Online:

  • Visit https://joinindiannavy.gov.in → Click on "Register" → Register as AVR (Apprentice).
  • After successful registration, click on "Login" → AVR (Apprentice) → INET 2/26 → Proceed to apply.
  • Fill in all details carefully. Incorrect declaration of information identified at any stage will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • Upload a recent passport-size colour photograph (taken not before May 2026), size 10 KB to 50 KB; front portrait in light background without head gear (except Sikhs). Date of photograph must be clearly written at the bottom.
  • A live photograph will be captured through the webcam during application form filling.
  • Pay the examination fee of ₹550/- + 18% GST online via net banking / Credit Card / Debit Card / UPI.
  • Give state preference for allocation of INET exam centre. Choice of centre cannot be changed once selected or allotted by the Indian Navy.
  • Candidates will be provided a post-closing correction window. An additional correction window will be activated post INET exam for 2 days.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:

  • Matriculation Certificate and Marksheet (proof of date of birth and educational qualification)
  • Diploma Certificate and Consolidated Marksheet from AICTE-recognised Polytechnic Institute
  • Domicile Certificate
  • NCC 'C' Certificate (if applicable) - to be verified through DG NCC
  • Sponsorship Certificate (if applicable) - to be verified through CABS
  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (taken not before May 2026)

Documents to be Produced at INS Chilka at the time of Induction:

  • All original certificates as submitted/uploaded during online application
  • Self-attested copies of all certificates
  • Original NCC 'C' Certificate / Sponsorship Certificate (if held)
  • Police Verification Form duly verified by the Superintendent of Police of respective jurisdiction (from place of domicile or place of residence)
  • Call-up letter / Admit Card

Apply Online at: https://joinindiannavy.gov.in → Register → AVR (Apprentice) → INET 2/26

Call-up Letters cum Admit Card for Stage I - INET 2/2026 will be available for download from https://joinindiannavy.gov.in approximately one week before the online examination. No call-up letter will be sent by post. Only Electronic mode of communication will be used for contacting candidates.

Indian Navy Agniveer (Apprentice) Recruitment 2026 - Important Instructions

  • Only unmarried Indian male candidates are eligible. Agniveers cannot marry during their entire 4-year tenure; violation will result in dismissal from service.
  • Agniveers are enrolled under the Navy Act 1957 for a period of 4 years. Indian Navy is not obliged to retain Agniveers beyond the engagement period.
  • Up to 25% of each specific batch of Agniveers may be offered permanent enrolment in the Indian Navy as Sailors (Regular Cadre) / Artificers based on performance and organisational requirements. Selection for further enrolment is entirely at the discretion of the Indian Navy; Agniveers have no right to be selected.
  • Agniveers will not be permitted to seek release at their own request prior to completion of the engagement period.
  • Agniveers are not governed by Naval Pension Regulations/Rules and are not entitled to gratuity for the engagement period.
  • Agniveers will not be eligible for Ex-Servicemen status.
  • Candidates declaring incorrect domicile/gender and other details shall not be permitted to appear in the recruitment and their candidature will be cancelled.
  • Candidates reporting 30 minutes after the reporting time for Stage I - INET shall not be allowed to appear in the examination.
  • A candidate's selection pertaining to a particular batch is valid for that batch only. Qualified candidates whose names do not appear in the merit list cannot claim admission for the next batch.
  • Mobile phones or any other communication devices are not allowed inside the examination premises. Infringement will entail disciplinary action including ban from future examinations.
  • Candidates should not submit multiple applications. If more than one application is received, candidature will be cancelled.
  • Minimum height standard is 157 cms. Permanent body tattoos are only permitted on the inner face of forearms (inside of elbow to wrist) and on the reversed side of palm/back (dorsal) side of hand. Tattoos on any other part of the body are not acceptable.
  • Candidates are advised to get their ears cleaned for wax and tartar removed from teeth prior to medical examination.
  • Visual Standards: Uncorrected Vision 6/12, 6/12 | Corrected Vision 6/6, 6/6 | Colour Perception: CP PASS.
  • No enquiry will be entertained regarding this recruitment/enrolment after a period of six months post completion of the recruitment cycle for the respective batch.
  • The terms and conditions given in the advertisement are guidelines only and are subject to change without notice. Further, orders issued by the Government as amended from time to time will apply for selected candidates.

Check complete details for Railway RRB ALP CEN 01/2026 Notification 2026. Includes important dates, application link, eligibility, syllabus and result info — updated live on OnlyJobs.

⚡ Key Highlights

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) have officially announced the Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) Recruitment 2026 with 11,127 openings. The detailed notification was published on 14 May 2026. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18 to 30 years and hold Matriculation + ITI or Diploma/Degree in Engineering can now submit their online applications from 15 May 2026 to 14 June 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

FOLLOW US

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Railway Recruitment Boards have officially released Centralised Employment Notice (CEN) No. 01/2026 for recruitment to 11127 posts of Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) under various Railway Zones, Government of India. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, examination syllabus, medical standards, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification and its annexures before applying online. The notification PDF is available on all official RRB websites.

  • Download RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs), Ministry of Railways, Government of India
Advertisement No. CEN No. 01/2026
Post Name Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP)
Department Ministry of Railways, Government of India (Various Zonal Railways)
Total Vacancies 11127 (All RRBs combined)
Pay Scale Level-2 in 7th CPC Pay Matrix; Initial Pay Rs. 19,900/-
Medical Standard A-1 (Stringent vision and physical fitness standards; Lasik surgery candidates NOT eligible)
Application Mode Online only (via official RRB websites)
Selection Mode CBT-1 (75 marks) + CBT-2 (Part A: 100 marks + Part B: 75 marks qualifying) + CBAT + Document Verification + Medical Examination
Suitability for PwBD Not Suitable
Official Website Refer RRB-wise websites listed below (e.g., www.rrbald.gov.in, www.rrbmumbai.gov.in, etc.)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Indicative Notice in Employment News 21/03/2026
Opening of Online Application Portal 15-05-2026 (00:00 Hrs.)
Last Date for Online Application Submission 14-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Last Date for Application Fee Payment 16-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Modification Window for Application Corrections 17-06-2026 to 26-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Age Calculated As On 01-07-2026
CBT-1 / CBT-2 / CBAT Date To be notified on official RRB websites

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 11127 vacancies have been announced for the post of Assistant Loco Pilot across all Railway Recruitment Boards and Zonal Railways. These are distributed across UR, SC, ST, OBC, and EWS categories. Ex-Servicemen (ExSM) vacancies are included within the total vacancy count and are not separately additional. The post is not suitable for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD).

Category No. of Posts
UR (Unreserved) 4860
SC (Scheduled Caste) 1746
ST (Scheduled Tribe) 919
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 2598
EWS (Economically Weaker Section) 1004
Total 11127
Ex-Servicemen (ExSM) - included in above total 1115

RRB & Zone-wise Vacancy Details (Selected RRBs)

RRB Zone UR SC ST OBC EWS Total ExSM
Ahmedabad WR 62 22 11 38 14 147 15
Ajmer NWR + WCR 318 115 59 174 52 718 72
Mumbai CR + SCR + WR 336 219 106 380 152 1193 120
Bhubaneswar ECoR 548 70 36 48 99 801 80
Guwahati NFR 272 109 63 164 68 676 68
Secunderabad ECoR + SCR 625 209 102 390 94 1420 143
Ranchi ECR + SER 472 233 105 363 127 1300 130
All RRBs Total 4860 1746 919 2598 1004 11127 1115

Note: Complete RRB & Zone-wise vacancy details are available in Annexure B of the official notification.

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (14-06-2026). Educational/Technical qualification certificates must be from a recognized Board/University/Institute. Candidates awaiting results of their final examination for the prescribed qualification are NOT eligible to apply. The post of ALP is not suitable for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD). Candidates who have undergone Lasik Surgery or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for this post.

Educational Qualification

Option Details
Option A - Matriculation + ITI Matriculation / SSLC plus ITI from recognised institutions of NCVT/SCVT in the trades of Fitter, Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV), Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Wireman, Tractor Mechanic, Armature & Coil Winder, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Turner, Machinist, Refrigeration & Air-Conditioning Mechanic. OR Matriculation / SSLC plus Course Completed Act Apprenticeship in the above trades.
Option B - Matriculation + Diploma/Degree in Engineering Matriculation / SSLC plus 3-year Diploma in Mechanical / Electrical / Electronics / Automobile Engineering (or combination of these streams) from a recognised institution in lieu of ITI. Note: Degree in the above Engineering disciplines will also be acceptable in lieu of Diploma.

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be between 18 and 30 years of age as on 01-07-2026. Age relaxation is available for various categories as detailed below. If a candidate is eligible for relaxation on more than one ground, only the maximum relaxation will be granted (not cumulative). SC, ST, and OBC (NCL) candidates applying against UR vacancies will not get any age relaxation. There is no fixed limit on the number of attempts - candidates can appear as long as they are within the prescribed upper age limit.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) & EWS 18 to 30 years (as on 01-07-2026)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (max 33 years)
SC & ST 5 years relaxation (max 35 years)
Ex-Servicemen (UR & EWS) 3 years after deduction of length of service from age
Ex-Servicemen (OBC-NCL) 6 years after deduction of length of service from age
Ex-Servicemen (SC & ST) 8 years after deduction of length of service from age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - UR & EWS Maximum 40 years of age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - OBC (NCL) Maximum 43 years of age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - SC & ST Maximum 45 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (UR & EWS) Maximum 35 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (OBC-NCL) Maximum 38 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (SC & ST) Maximum 40 years of age

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay the non-refundable recruitment fee online. Fee can be paid only through online mode - internet banking, debit/credit cards, or UPI. Applications received without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected. Candidates who appear in CBT-1 will receive a refund of their examination fee (after deducting applicable bank charges) credited to their Aadhaar-seeded bank account. Candidates whose applications are rejected, or who do not appear in CBT-1, will not receive any refund.

Category Examination Fee Refund on Appearing in CBT-1
All Candidates (except categories below) Rs. 500/- (Rupees Five Hundred only) Rs. 400/- refunded (deducting bank charges)
SC / ST / Ex-Servicemen / Female / Transgender / Minorities / Economically Backward Class (EBC) Rs. 250/- (Rupees Two Hundred and Fifty only) Rs. 250/- fully refunded (deducting bank charges)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 consists of five successive stages - First Stage CBT (CBT-1), Second Stage CBT (CBT-2), Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT), Document Verification (DV), and Medical Examination (ME). CBT-1 is a screening exam only and its marks are not counted in the final panel. The final merit list is prepared on the basis of combined marks from Part-A of CBT-2 (50% weightage) and CBAT scores (50% weightage). Negative marking of 1/3rd mark per wrong answer applies in both CBT-1 and CBT-2. There is no negative marking in CBAT.

Stage 1 - First Stage CBT (CBT-1) - Screening Only

Details Particulars
Duration 60 Minutes
Total Questions / Marks 75 Questions / 75 Marks (1 mark per question)
Type Objective MCQ - Computer Based Test
Negative Marking 1/3rd mark deducted per wrong answer
Minimum Qualifying Marks UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Shortlisting for CBT-2 15 times the vacancies notified per RRB (based on CBT-1 merit, RRB-wise)
Syllabus Mathematics, Mental Ability (Reasoning), General Science (10th standard level)

Stage 2 - Second Stage CBT (CBT-2)

Part Duration Questions Nature Minimum Qualifying Marks
Part-A 90 Minutes 100 Questions / 100 Marks Competitive - marks counted for merit UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Part-B 60 Minutes 75 Questions / 75 Marks Qualifying only - trade-specific 35% for all candidates irrespective of category
Total Duration 175 Questions / 2.5 Hours  

Part-A Syllabus (CBT-2): Mathematics, General Intelligence and Reasoning, Basic Science and Engineering (Engineering Drawing, Units, Measurements, Basic Electricity, Levers, IT Literacy, etc.)

Part-B Syllabus (CBT-2) - Trade-wise Qualifying Test:

Sl. No. Qualification of Candidate Trade / Subject of Choice for Part-B
1 Electrical Engineering (and combinations) Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Wireman, Armature & Coil Winder, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic
2 Electronics Engineering (and combinations) Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV)
3 Mechanical Engineering (and combinations) Fitter, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Turner, Machinist, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic, Heat Engine, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic
4 Automobile Engineering (and combinations) Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic

Stage 3 - Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT)

Shortlisting for CBAT 8 times the ALP vacancies per community/category per RRB (based on Part-A marks of CBT-2)
Qualifying Marks Minimum T-score of 42 in each test battery separately (all candidates irrespective of category)
Language English and Hindi only
Negative Marking No negative marking

Stage 4 & 5 - Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination (ME)

Final merit list is prepared RRB-wise from candidates qualifying in CBAT, with 50% weightage to Part-A of CBT-2 marks and 50% weightage to CBAT scores. Candidates equal to the number of vacancies are shortlisted for DV and Medical Examination. In case of a tie, the older candidate is preferred; if age is also equal, alphabetical order of given name (A to Z) is applied.

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Seniority of age (older candidate preferred)
2nd Tie-Break Alphabetical order (A to Z) of given name (not surname)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Medical Standards

The post of ALP carries Medical Standard A-1. Candidates must be physically fit in all respects with stringent vision requirements. Candidates who have undergone Lasik Surgery or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible. A Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Part-A signed by General Physician and Part-B signed by Eye Specialist, both from a Central/State Government Hospital or Medical College) must be submitted on the CBAT exam day.

Medical Standard General Fitness Vision Standards
A-1 Physically fit in all respects (i) Distant Vision: 6/6, 6/6 without glasses with fogging test (must not accept +2D); (ii) Near Vision: Sn: 0.6, 0.6 without glasses; (iii) Must pass tests for Colour Vision, Binocular Vision, Field of Vision, Night Vision, Mesopic Vision, etc.

How to Apply for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the official websites of participating RRBs (listed at Para 13.7 of the CEN). Hard copy / offline applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. The online portal is active from 15-05-2026 to 14-06-2026 (23:59 Hrs.). Candidates must first create an account on the RRB portal. Those who created an account for CENs notified in 2024/2025 should use the same credentials. Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and the chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation. Candidates can apply to only one RRB and submit only one application - multiple applications to the same or different RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and debarment from future RRB/RRC exams. Aadhaar or DigiLocker authentication is strongly advised during account creation for a smoother recruitment process. A live photograph is captured during the online application; pre-existing photographs are not accepted. No documents need to be uploaded at application stage (except signature and SC/ST certificate for free travel pass, if applicable).

Documents to be Produced at Document Verification (on being called):

  • Age proof - Matriculation / SSLC / 10th class certificate
  • Educational qualifications (Matriculation onwards) - marksheets and certificates
  • ITI / Diploma / Degree certificate in prescribed trade / discipline
  • SC / ST / OBC-NCL / EWS / Ex-Servicemen certificate (if applicable), in prescribed format and issued by competent authority
  • Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Annexure VIII A & VIII B) - to be submitted on CBAT day
  • NOC from present employer (if applicable, for Government servants)

Candidates are advised to regularly visit the official RRB website for their chosen RRB for updates on all stages. Helpline for technical queries: Email: [email protected] | Phone: 0172-565-3333 and 9592001188 (10:00 AM to 5:00 PM on working days).

Apply Online at: Visit the official website of your chosen RRB (see list below)

RRB-wise Official Websites for CEN 01/2026

RRB Official Website
Ahmedabad www.rrbahmedabad.gov.in
Ajmer www.rrbajmer.gov.in
Bhopal www.rrbbhopal.gov.in
Bhubaneswar www.rrbbbs.gov.in
Bilaspur www.rrbbilaspur.gov.in
Chandigarh www.rrbcdg.gov.in
Chennai www.rrbchennai.gov.in
Gorakhpur www.rrbgkp.gov.in
Guwahati www.rrbguwahati.gov.in
Jammu-Srinagar www.rrbjammu.nic.in
Kolkata www.rrbkolkata.gov.in
Malda www.rrbmalda.gov.in
Mumbai www.rrbmumbai.gov.in
Muzaffarpur www.rrbmuzaffarpur.gov.in
Patna www.rrbpatna.gov.in
Prayagraj www.rrbald.gov.in
Ranchi www.rrbranchi.gov.in
Secunderabad www.rrbsecunderabad.gov.in
Siliguri www.rrbsiliguri.gov.in
Thiruvananthapuram www.rrbthiruvananthapuram.gov.in

Important Instructions for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only one application per candidate is accepted - multiple applications to one or more RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and permanent debarment from RRB/RRC exams.
  • Candidates awaiting results of their final prescribed qualification examination are NOT eligible to apply.
  • Candidates who have undergone Lasik or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for the post of ALP.
  • Selection of RRB, once submitted, is final and cannot be changed at any later date.
  • Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation.
  • Examination fee is refundable (after deducting bank charges) only to candidates who appear in CBT-1; rejected or absent candidates are not eligible for refund.
  • Refund will be credited only to the Aadhaar-seeded bank account; candidates must ensure their Aadhaar is linked to an active bank account.
  • Mobile phones, electronic gadgets, watches, earphones, Bluetooth devices, calculators, books, pen, food items, and all other listed banned items are strictly prohibited inside the test centre.
  • Examination centre, date, and shift allotted in the e-call letter are final - no requests for change will be entertained under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must maintain identical signatures throughout all stages of the recruitment process. No BLOCK/CAPITAL/DISJOINTED letter signatures are acceptable.
  • Free Sleeper Class Railway Pass is available for SC/ST candidates for CBT/CBAT/DV/Medical Examination stages (if opted in application).
  • Candidates are advised to visit only official RRB websites and beware of fake websites, unscrupulous agents, and touts claiming to provide Railway jobs through influence or unfair means. RRBs never appoint agents or coaching centres for recruitment.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL), a Government of India Enterprise, has released an official advertisement for 100 posts of Junior Telecom Officer – Direct Recruitment (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR] from the open market.

This recruitment is under Advt. No. BSNLCO-11/12(11)/1/2026-RECTT-CO dated May 26, 2026. Both internal and external candidates fulfilling the prescribed eligibility conditions are eligible to apply. The deadline for online registration is July 3, 2026 at 10:00 AM.

FOLLOW US

BSNL JTO Vacancy and Salary Highlights

Essential administrative details regarding the vacancy package include:

Parameter Details
Post Name Junior Telecom Officer – Direct Recruitment (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR]
Total Vacancies 100
Pay Scale IDA Pay Scale [E-1] of ₹16,400 – ₹40,500/- (2nd PRC pay scale w.e.f. 01.01.2007)
Probation Period Two Years
Bond ₹6 Lakhs bond to serve BSNL for a minimum of 5 years from date of appointment

Eligibility Criteria & Job Requirements

Candidates must fulfill the minimum qualifications specified for the Telecom stream:

Educational Qualification

  • Core Qualification (Telecom Stream): Bachelor of Engineering Degree or equivalent from a Central Govt./State Govt. recognized Institution/University in any of the following disciplines: Telecommunications, Electronics, Radio, Computer, Electrical, Information Technology (IT), or Instrumentation.
  • Alternative Qualification: M.Sc. (Electronics) or M.Sc. (Computer Science).
  • Higher Qualifications: Candidates possessing M.Tech. in the prescribed disciplines are also eligible to appear in the Direct Recruitment examination.
  • Vision Requirement: The applicant must be capable of distinguishing principal colours and should have binocular vision.
  • Nationality: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.

Age Limit & Category-wise Relaxation

General Age Limit: Minimum 20 years and maximum 30 years as on the closing date of receipt of application.

Application Fee

Category Examination Fee
UR / OBC / EWS Candidates ₹2,000/-

Fee must be paid through online mode only (Internet Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card) on the BSNL website: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in. Fee once paid is non-refundable.

BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 Selection Methodology

The selection process is conducted through a Computer Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test (CBT) as follows:

  1. Written Examination (CBT): 3 hours duration, comprising 120 questions for a total of 480 marks across three sections.
Section Particulars Questions Maximum Marks
Section I Engineering Stream Section – I 50 Questions 200 Marks
Section II Engineering Stream Section – II 50 Questions 200 Marks
Section III General Ability Test Section – III 20 Questions 80 Marks
Total 120 Questions 480 Marks
  1. Marking Scheme: +4 marks for each correct answer and -1 mark for each wrong answer (negative marking applicable).
  2. Minimum Qualifying Marks: Minimum 40% in each section individually. Overall minimum qualifying marks in all three sections combined: 50% for Unreserved/OBC (CL) candidates; 45% for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PwBD/EWS candidates.
  3. Document Verification: Selected candidates in the declared merit list will be called for document verification. Date and venue will be intimated in due course.
  4. Medical Examination: Appointment of selected candidates will be subject to passing BSNL's Medical Examination as per standards laid down by BSNL.

How to Apply for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must complete the online registration process as follows:

  1. Visit the BSNL official examination website: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in and register online only. No manual/paper applications will be entertained.
  2. Keep the following ready before registering: valid mobile number and email ID (for OTP), scanned passport size colour photograph (jpg/jpeg, 20–50 kb, white background), scanned signature (jpg/jpeg, 10–20 kb), Class 10th Certificate (date of birth proof), Degree Certificate and consolidated marksheet, photo identity proof (Aadhaar/Passport/Driving License/Voter ID), valid category/reservation certificate as applicable, and Medical Certificate for PwBD candidates.
  3. Pay the requisite non-refundable examination fee online through Internet Banking, Credit Card, or Debit Card.
  4. After completing registration, take a printout of the computer-generated registration form and retain it for future reference.
  5. An editing window will be open for 7 days after the registration period closes (July 4–11, 2026). Selective fields can be edited during this period only.
  6. Candidates currently employed in Government Departments/Public Sector Undertakings/Autonomous Bodies must produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their present employer at the time of document verification.
  7. Candidates must select up to 21 Circle preferences (in order of preference) from the registration portal dropdown menu. Options once exercised shall be final.
  8. Admit cards can be downloaded from the BSNL website using the registered username and password well before the examination date.

Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Bank of Baroda (BOB), Mumbai
Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01 dated 19/05/2026

Department Human Resource Management (HRM), Bank of Baroda

Stipend Rs. 15,000/- per month (no other allowances/benefits)

Application Mode Online only (via NATS / NAPS portals + BFSI SSC payment link)
Selection Mode Online Examination + Document Verification + Local Language Test
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration on NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in)
Official Website https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/ and https://bfsissc.com

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date

Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test Date To be notified after Online Examination result

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 5000 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank.

Multiple Disabilities Disability amongst clauses above including deaf-blindness in the posts identified for each disability

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Candidates must be registered on Government Apprenticeship Portals (NATS and/or NAPS) before applying. Only Indian citizens are eligible to apply.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Candidates must be registered on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and/or NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) before applying.
Educational Qualification Graduation Degree in any Discipline from a recognized University or any equivalent qualifications recognized by the Central Government. For NATS candidates: date of passing Graduation should not be more than 4 years preceding 01-05-2026.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship with Bank of Baroda or any other Establishment in the past, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training with any Establishment.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after completing educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
State/UT Application Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only and must select up to 3 preferred training locations/centres from those notified within that State/UT.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years of age as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for reserved categories, subject to the condition that NAPS-registered candidates should be below 34 years as on 01-05-2026.

Category Age Relaxation (in years)
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe 5
Other Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer) 3
PwBD – General / EWS 10
PwBD – OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 13
PwBD – SC / ST 15

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Application/Intimation/Examination Charges/Fees to BFSI SSC through the secure payment link provided in the application form. The fee must be paid through ONLINE mode only. No other mode of payment will be accepted. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account.

Category Total Fee / Charges
Scheduled Caste (SC) / Scheduled Tribe (ST) Candidates Rs. 200/- plus GST
Person with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) Candidates Rs. 200/- plus GST
General / EWS / OBC – Male Candidates Rs. 800/- plus GST
General / EWS / OBC – Female / Transgender Candidates Rs. 600/- plus GST

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on three stages - Online Examination, Document Verification, and Test of local language of the State. Candidates must qualify all three stages. Final selection is subject to medical fitness as per the Bank's requirements. A State-wise and Category-wise Wait List will be published for vacancies arising from non-acceptance or non-reporting by selected candidates; this wait list remains valid for 1 year from result declaration or until the next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier.

Stage 1 - Online Examination

S No Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 60 Minutes Hindi / English
2 Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude 25 25 Hindi / English
3 Computer Knowledge 25 25 Hindi / English
4 General English 25 25 English
Total 100 100  

There will be NO negative marks for wrong answers. The online examination will be remotely proctored - candidates can appear from any location of their choice using a smart mobile phone / laptop / desktop / tablet with an operational front camera, microphone, and stable Wi-Fi internet connection. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order of aggregate marks. Tie-break: higher age is preferred.

Stage 2 - Document Verification

Candidates qualifying the online examination will be called for Document Verification at a Bank-designated centre of the State/UT applied for, at their own expense. Bank of Baroda will not reimburse travelling expenses for Document Verification.

Stage 3 - Test of Local Language (State-wise)

Candidates who produce 10th or 12th standard mark sheet having studied the specified local language of the State/UT will not be required to undergo the language test. Candidates who fail to qualify the local language test will not be eligible for engagement as apprentice.

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register themselves on the Government Apprenticeship portals - NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in).

After completing registration on NAPS/NATS, eligible candidates will receive an email from [email protected] inviting them to fill the final "Application cum Examination Form" and make payment of the applicable fee/charges.

The application registration is considered complete only when fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC. Candidates must note down their Enrolment Id (NATS portal) and Apprentice Registration Code (NAPS portal) for all future correspondence.

Documents Required for Registration on NATS/NAPS Portals:

  • Colour scan copy of Aadhaar Card – Front & Back Page (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of PAN Card (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Valid Personal Email ID and Mobile Number
  • Colour scan copy of Passport-size Photograph (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Xth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of XIIth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Degree Marksheet / Provisional Certificate (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Saving Bank Passbook / Cheque Leaf (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Signature (JPEG, <1 MB)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test:

  • Printout of valid Language Proficiency Test Intimation Letter/mail from Bank
  • Valid system generated printout of the online application / Registration / Enrollment form
  • Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate from Competent Municipal Authority or SSLC / Std. X Certificate with DOB)
  • Photo Identity Proof (Government-issued ID)
  • Individual Semester/Year-wise Marksheets and Certificates for educational qualifications including final degree/diploma certificate
  • CGPA to Percentage conversion certificate (if grades awarded), issued by the appropriate authority
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates) - OBC-NCL certificate must be issued within one year as on date of advertisement
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
  • Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

Documents to be produced at the venue of Document Verification / Language Test. Non-submission of requisite documents will debar candidature from further participation in the selection process. Candidates must appear at their own expense.

Apply Online at: https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/ and https://bfsissc.com

Important Instructions for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • This is NOT regular employment or contract employment in the Bank. Apprentices will be TRAINEES only and not employees of Bank of Baroda.
  • The apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training. Bank of Baroda has no obligation to offer regular employment to apprentices during or after the apprenticeship period.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only. Allotment to a different State/UT will not be considered under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must first register on NATS and/or NAPS portals before applying; application without portal registration is not valid.
  • The process of registration is complete only when applicable fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC.
  • No Ex-servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an apprentice; there is no reservation for Ex-servicemen in this engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the 'Creamy Layer' are not entitled to OBC reservation and age relaxation; they should apply as General / General (OC/HI/VI/ID) as applicable.
  • Change of category once submitted in the online application will not be permitted at any stage.
  • Candidates should submit only one application; in case of multiple submissions, only the last valid completed application will be considered and fee paid for earlier registrations will be forfeited.
  • The Apprentices are prohibited from joining / forming any Union Body.
  • All correspondence will be made only on the registered email ID; candidates should keep it active throughout the recruitment process. No hard copy intimation will be sent by post for Online Examination.
  • Candidates must appear for the selection process (Document Verification, Language Test) at their own cost; Bank will not reimburse travelling expenses.
  • Apprentices will not be provided with Transport / Hostel facility or any other residential accommodation during training.
  • Stipend of Rs. 15,000/- per month will be paid on completion of each month after adjusting Loss of Pay, if any. No other allowances/benefits are payable.
  • All amendments/corrigendum (if any) will be published only on the Bank's website (https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/) and https://bfsissc.com - candidates should visit regularly.
  • Any canvassing directly or indirectly shall disqualify the candidature. Use of unfair means, impersonation, or misbehavior will lead to disqualification and/or debarment.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.

RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card 2026 OUT - Download Hall Ticket

RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card 2026 has been released by the Reserve Bank of India on 27 May 2026. Candidates who qualified the Prelims can now download their call letter from the official website opportunities.rbi.org.in. The Mains examination is scheduled for 07 June 2026 across various centers in India. This hall ticket is mandatory for appearing in the exam. How to Download RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official RBI website at opportunities.rbi.org.in or click the direct download link above.
  2. Step 2: Navigate to the “Call Letters” section under Current Vacancies.
  3. Step 3: Click on “Recruitment for the Post of Assistant in Reserve Bank of India - Panel Year 2026: Call Letter for Online Main Examination”.
  4. Step 4: Enter your credentials:
    • Registration Number / Roll Number
    • Password / Date of Birth
    • Captcha Code
  5. Step 5: Click on “Submit” button.
  6. Step 6: Your RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen.
  7. Step 7: Download and save the PDF file on your device.
  8. Step 8: Take 2-3 printouts for exam day (keep backup copies).

Pro Tip: Check all details on your admit card immediately after download. Report any discrepancies to RBI helpdesk immediately.

  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one):
    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones, smartwatches
  • Calculators, electronic devices

Important Instructions for RBI Assistant Mains Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card well in advance
  • Verify exam center location
  • Keep all documents ready

On Exam Day:

  • Reach the center 45-60 minutes early
  • Follow all invigilator instructions
  • No entry after gate closure

What If RBI Assistant Mains Admit Card Not Available?

If you face issues downloading:

  1. Clear browser cache and try again.
  2. Use a different browser (Chrome recommended).
  3. Check your Prelims result status.
  4. Contact RBI helpline immediately.

The latest Punjab & Sind Bank (PSB) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Apprentice posts! Published on 21-05-2026 at the official website of Punjab & Sind Bank, this new recruitment drive offers 635 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 20 to 28 years age group and possessing a Regular Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute can apply online from 21-05-2026 to 06-06-2026. Find all details about the Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Punjab & Sind Bank (A Govt. of India Undertaking), H.O. HRD Department, NBCC Building Block 3, Kidwai Nagar, New Delhi – 110023 has officially released a notification dated 21/05/2026 for engagement of 635 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise and district-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official Punjab & Sind Bank website.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

Punjab & Sind Bank is recruiting 635 Apprentice posts with Regular Graduation qualification, last date 06 June 2026.

Organization Name Punjab & Sind Bank (A Govt. of India Undertaking), H.O. HRD Department, New Delhi

A total of 635 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the respective cut-off dates. Only candidates with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals are eligible to apply through the Bank's website. Candidates can apply for only one State and one District and must not submit more than one application.

Criteria Details
Nationality / Citizenship Citizen of India / Subject of Nepal or Bhutan / Tibetan Refugee (came before 01-01-1962) / Person of Indian origin migrated from specified countries - with eligibility certificate from Govt. of India where applicable.
Portal Registration (Mandatory) 100% completed profile on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/) before applying on PSB website.
Educational Qualification (as on 06-06-2026) Regular Graduation in any discipline from a recognized University/Institute or equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government.
Post Graduate Candidates Candidates who have completed Post Graduate qualification are NOT eligible.
Work Experience Candidates with work experience of 1 year or more after educational qualification are NOT eligible.
Years Since Qualifying Exam Candidates should not have completed 5 years after passing the qualifying examination.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidates who have already undergone or are currently undergoing Apprenticeship in any organisation under the Apprentices Act 1961 are NOT eligible.
Local Language Proficiency The candidate must be proficient in the local language (Reading, Writing, Speaking & Understanding) of the applied State/UT and must produce 8th/10th/12th or Graduate level certificate evidencing having studied the local language.
Aadhaar Requirement Every apprentice must have a valid Aadhaar number before engagement / contract signing.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-05-2026. Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) / EWS 20 to 28 years (as on 01-05-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation
Persons With Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) 10 years relaxation
Widows / Divorced Women / Women legally separated (not remarried) Up to 35 years (General/EWS), 38 years (OBC), 40 years (SC/ST)
Persons affected by 1984 riots 5 years relaxation

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

Category Fees / Intimation Charges
SC / ST / PwBD Rs. 100/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges
General / EWS / OBC Rs. 200/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be merit-based on HSC/10+2 marks. No written examination is conducted. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility, district-wise vacancy availability, and medical fitness. In case of a tie in marks, candidates will be ranked by age in descending order within their respective category, state, and district.

Stage 1 - Merit List (Based on HSC / 10+2 Marks)

Candidates who fulfill minimum eligibility criteria will be placed in a merit list in descending order of their 10+2 marks, drawn State-wise, District-wise, and Category-wise. No written examination is conducted for this recruitment. Candidates must select their preferred State and District in the application itself. In case of equal marks, tie-break is by age in descending order within the respective category, state, and district.

Stage 2 - Document Verification

Shortlisted candidates will be intimated through email only and must appear for document verification at their own expense. Original documents along with self-attested photocopies must be produced. Candidates failing to produce originals will not be allowed to proceed further.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - District-wise Vacancy Details

Candidates must apply for one specific district within their chosen State. District-wise allotment will be subject to vacancy availability and administrative requirements of the Bank.

Competition Level Analysis for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The competition level for PSB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be moderate to high. With only 635 seats spread across 29 States/UTs, the vacancy count is significantly lower than comparable bank apprenticeship drives such as SBI. Selection is purely merit-based on 10+2 marks without any written examination, which means candidates with high HSC scores - especially from states like Uttar Pradesh (100 seats), Punjab (90 seats), Bihar (40 seats), Maharashtra (30 seats), and Haryana (50 seats) - will face intense competition within their district and category.

Since there is no written exam, the effective competition depends entirely on the candidate's 10+2 percentage and age as a tie-breaker. Attrition at later stages will occur through local language proof verification, document verification, and medical fitness, which will reduce the final eligible pool. Candidates who do not have proof of studying the local language at 8th/10th/12th level are at a disadvantage as they must demonstrate local language proficiency separately.

Who should NOT apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent job; this is a 12-month apprenticeship only, with no guarantee of absorption or regular employment in the Bank.
  • Candidates who have already completed any Apprenticeship (in PSB or any other organisation) or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act.
  • Candidates who have completed Post Graduate (PG) qualification - PG holders are explicitly not eligible.
  • Candidates with 1 year or more of work/job experience after completion of educational qualification.
  • Candidates who have completed more than 5 years after passing their qualifying examination.
  • Candidates outside the age band of 20–28 years as on 01-05-2026, except those eligible for notified relaxation under SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/widow/1984 riot victim categories.
  • Aspirants who do not hold a Regular Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute by 06-06-2026.
  • Candidates not proficient in the local language of the State/UT they are applying for, and who cannot produce 8th/10th/12th or graduation-level certificate evidencing study of that language.
  • Candidates unwilling or unable to register with 100% completed profile on both NAPS (apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and NATS (nats.education.gov.in) portals before applying - this is a mandatory prerequisite.
  • Candidates without a valid Aadhaar number, as it is mandatory before engagement/contract signing.
  • Candidates applying with a Ration Card or Learner's Driving License as ID proof - these are not accepted as valid identity documents for this process.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Not completing 100% profile on both NAPS and NATS portals before applying; incomplete portal profiles will render the candidate ineligible.
  • Applying for a wrong State or District where the candidate is not proficient in the local language and cannot produce language study certificates.
  • Entering the wrong category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR); category cannot be changed after submission and any mismatch at document verification can lead to cancellation.
  • OBC candidates from the creamy layer applying under OBC reservation instead of UR; only Non-Creamy Layer OBC candidates with a valid, recent certificate are entitled to reservation.
  • Uploading blurred or incorrect photograph, signature, thumb impression or handwritten declaration (wrong size, format, or written by someone else) leading to rejection at scrutiny.
  • Using an invalid or inactive mobile number or email ID, resulting in failure to receive OTPs, shortlisting intimations, or further communication from the Bank.
  • Submitting multiple applications; only the last valid completed application will be retained and fees paid for other registrations will be forfeited.
  • Signing the application in CAPITAL LETTERS; signatures in capital letters are not acceptable and must match across all documents and correspondence.
  • Forgetting to note down the Enrollment ID generated on the NATS portal, which is mandatory to be mentioned while applying for training seats.
  • Not reading the clause that this is not regular employment, and later disputing about permanent absorption after the 12-month apprenticeship ends.

How to Apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online through the Bank's website (https://punjabandsind.bank.in/) only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates must mandatorily complete 100% registration on both the NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and the NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/). Candidates must also note down their Enrollment ID generated on the NATS portal as it is required while applying.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (to be signed across by the candidate on printing)
  • Signature with black ink on white paper (Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be accepted)
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink - text: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."
  • Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at time of online application)
  • Image files must be in .jpg / .jpeg format; scanned documents must be in PDF format only

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Joining:

  • Xth / Date of Birth certificate in support of Date of Birth
  • Printout of application form
  • All certificates and mark sheets of educational qualification from Standard Xth, XII, and Graduation onwards
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format for SC/ST candidates
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format, valid as on date of joining (issued within one year prior to joining); OBC Caste Name must tally with Central Government list
  • Income and Asset Certificate in prescribed format issued by competent authority, valid EWS certificate for the financial year, for EWS category candidates
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board for PwBD candidates
  • Age relaxation proof with valid documents (e.g., District Magistrate certificate for 1984 riot-affected persons)
  • Photo Identity Proof (PAN Card / Passport / Permanent Driving License / Voter’s Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Aadhaar / E-Aadhaar / Identity Card issued by recognised College/University / Gazetted Officer certificate) - bearing the same name as on the application / call letter. Ration Card and Learner’s Driving License are NOT valid.
  • Two passport-size photographs similar to the one used during portal registration
  • Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

NO DOCUMENT SHOULD BE DIRECTLY SENT TO THE BANK BEFORE OR AFTER THE PROCESS. Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to the Bank. Candidates must appear for document verification at their own expense.

Apply Online at: https://punjabandsind.bank.in/ → Home Page → Recruitment → Relevant Notification

For queries: Email [email protected] | All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only.

Important Instructions for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of the Bank and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for Bank employees.
  • The Bank shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to any Apprentice who has completed the period of training, nor shall the Apprentice be obligated to accept employment. The Apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training.
  • Candidates can apply for only one State and one District, and not more than one application should be submitted. In case of multiple applications, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained and fees for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • Mandatory registration with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals before applying on the Bank's website is required.
  • Candidates who had training or job experience of one or more years after completion of educational qualifications shall not be eligible for engagement as apprentice.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General/UR.
  • Change of address / email ID / phone number / caste / category will not be entertained after submission of the application.
  • Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be acceptable. The signature must be consistent across all documents and correspondence.
  • No daily allowance, conveyance allowance or travelling allowance will be paid to Apprentices. No overtime is permitted. Apprentices are entitled to only 1 casual leave per month (maximum 4 at a time).
  • The Contract of Apprenticeship shall terminate automatically on expiry of the 12-month training period. A non-performing apprentice will be given 7 days' notice. An apprentice wishing to terminate early must give 15 days' prior notice to the Bank.
  • Apprentices must devote their entire time exclusively to the Bank and shall not engage in any other commercial business, service, or advisory activity during the engagement period.
  • Apprentices must not disclose any financial information, data, concepts or internal communications relating to the Bank's business to any unauthorized person.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Any resultant dispute arising out of this advertisement shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of the Courts situated at Delhi.
  • Bank reserves the right to change/add/cancel the selection process or cancel the entire process at any stage without notice or refund of application fees.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only - candidates should visit regularly.
