📄 Complete Details & Notification

The State Bank of India (SBI) has officially announced the Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) - Specialist Cadre Officer Recruitment 2026 with 100 openings. The detailed notification was published on 13 May 2026 at sbi.co.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 23 to 32 years (relaxations applicable as per category) and hold a Graduate degree in any discipline from a recognized University along with Certification in Forex Operations from IIBF can now submit their online applications from 13 May 2026 to 02 June 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

FOLLOW US

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

State Bank of India, Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai has officially released Advertisement No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/05 dated 13.05.2026 for recruitment to 100 posts of Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) on Regular Basis under Specialist Cadre Officers. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, age limit, pay scale, job profile, selection process, application fee, and instructions to apply. The position shall remain in Specialist cadre and will have no provision for conversion into General cadre. Candidates are strongly advised to carefully read the full notification before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the Bank’s official career website.

  • Download SBI Trade Finance Officer SCO Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

SBI Trade Finance Officer SCO Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name State Bank of India (SBI), Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai
Advertisement No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/05 dated 13.05.2026
Post Name Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) – Specialist Cadre Officer, Regular Basis
Grade / Scale Middle Management Grade Scale – II (MMGS-II)
Total Vacancies 100 (Regular: 88 + Backlog: 12)
Pay Scale Rs. 64820-2340/1-67160-2680/10-93960
Suggested Place of Posting Hyderabad & Kolkata (indicative only; selected candidates may be posted anywhere in India)
Application Mode Online only (via https://sbi.co.in/web/careers/current-openings)
Selection Mode Shortlisting and Interview
Cut-off Date for Eligibility 30.04.2026
Official Website https://sbi.co.in

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 13.05.2026
Opening of Online Registration & Payment of Fees 13-05-2026
Last Date for Online Registration & Payment of Fees 02-06-2026
Eligibility Cut-off Date (Age, Qualification, Experience) 30-04-2026
Interview Date To be notified on SBI website
Call Letter for Interview Sent by email / uploaded on Bank’s website only (No hard copy)

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 100 vacancies have been announced for the post of Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) on regular basis, comprising 88 regular vacancies and 12 backlog vacancies. The vacancies include horizontal reservations for PwBD candidates. EWS vacancies are tentative and subject to further directives of Government of India and outcome of any litigation. The position shall remain in Specialist cadre with no provision for conversion into General cadre.

Post Type Total
Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) Regular 88
Backlog 12
Total 100

SC: Scheduled Caste | ST: Scheduled Tribe | OBC: Other Backward Class | EWS: Economically Weaker Section | UR: Unreserved | VI: Visually Impaired | HI: Hearing Impaired | LD: Locomotive Disability | d&e: Specific Learning Disability, Mental Illness, Autism spectrum disorder, Multiple disabilities. PwBD reservation is horizontal and included in overall vacancy of respective parent category.

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 30.04.2026. Teaching and training experience will not be counted for eligibility. The relevant experience certificate from the employer must specifically confirm that the candidate had experience in the related field as required. In cases where the degree/diploma certificate does not specify the field of specialization, a certificate from the concerned university/college specifically mentioning the specialization must be produced.

Educational Qualification and Experience

Qualification / Experience Details
Mandatory Educational Qualification Graduate (any discipline) from Government recognized University or Institution AND Certification in Forex Operations from IIBF (Certificate should be dated on or before 30.04.2026)
Preferred Qualification Certificate for Documentary Credit Specialists (CDCS) / Certificate in Trade Finance / Certificate in International Banking (Certificate should be dated on or before 30.04.2026)
Mandatory Experience Minimum 2 years’ post-essential academic qualification experience in Trade Finance processing in Supervisory/Official role in any Scheduled Commercial Bank
Specific Skills Required Outstanding communication, presentation and processing skills

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age is calculated as on 30.04.2026. Cumulative age relaxation will not be available. Candidates seeking age relaxation are required to submit copies of necessary certificate(s) at the time of interview, if shortlisted. No change in category of any candidate is permitted after online registration; no correspondence/email/phone will be entertained in this regard.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
Unreserved (UR) 23 to 32 years (as on 30.04.2026)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation
PwBD (UR / EWS) 10 years relaxation
PwBD (OBC) 13 years relaxation
PwBD (SC / ST) 15 years relaxation

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fee and Intimation Charges are non-refundable and must be paid online through the payment gateway integrated with the application (Debit Card/Credit Card/Internet Banking). Transaction charges for online payment, if any, will be borne by the candidate. Application fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be adjusted for any other examination or selection in future. In case of multiple applications, fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.

Category Application Fee
General / EWS / OBC Rs. 750/- (Rupees Seven Hundred Fifty only)
SC / ST / PwBD Nil (No fee / intimation charges)

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be on the basis of Shortlisting and Interview. Merely fulfilling the minimum qualification and experience will not vest any right to candidate for being called for interview. The shortlisting committee constituted by the Bank will decide the shortlisting parameters. The decision of the Bank to call the candidates for interview shall be final. Interview will carry 100 marks; qualifying marks will be decided by the Bank. Merit list for selection will be prepared in descending order on the basis of scores obtained in interview only. In case of tie, candidates will be ranked according to their age in descending order. Intimation/Call Letter for Interview will be sent by email or uploaded on Bank’s website only - no hard copy will be sent.

Stage Details
Stage 1 Shortlisting (based on qualification, suitability, and experience as decided by the Bank’s shortlisting committee)
Stage 2 Interview (100 marks; qualifying marks decided by the Bank)
Merit List Prepared in descending order of interview scores; tie-breaking by age in descending order
Probation Period 6 months (as per Bank’s extant recruitment policy)

Grade, Pay Scale & Perquisites

Grade Probation Period Pay Scale Perquisites
Middle Management Grade Scale – II (MMGS-II) 6 Months Rs. 64820-2340/1-67160-2680/10-93960 DA, HRA, CCA, Provident Fund, NPS (Contributory Pension Fund), Leave Fare Concession (LFC), Medical Facility, and other perquisites as per rules in force from time to time

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Job Profile & Key Responsibility Areas

The Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) position is in the Specialist cadre with no provision for conversion into General cadre. Suggested place of posting is Hyderabad and Kolkata; however, selected candidates may be posted anywhere in India at the sole discretion of the Bank. Requests for transfer/allotment to any centre other than Kolkata/Hyderabad shall not be entertained.

Area Details
Job Profile Trade Finance Document Scrutiny (LC/Collections/Bank Guarantees) for compliance with Bank/RBI/ICC/FEDAI/regulatory guidelines; Processing of Trade Finance and Forex transactions at a centralized location; Reconciliation of Trade Finance and Forex related entries in Bank’s Trade Finance, Forex and CBS systems and IDPMS/EDPMS; Any other tasks related to Bank’s Trade Finance and International Business back-office operations.
Key Responsibility Areas Accurate and efficient processing of all allotted Trade Finance and/or Forex transactions within benchmarked TAT; Tracking and ensuring periodic activities including maturities, payments, prompt follow-up, customer reconciliation; Timely reconciliation of related accounts/systems; Work closely with all stakeholders to maintain agreed customer TAT; Ensure adherence to all regulatory/bank/ICC/FEDAI guidelines; Participation in RCSA, BCOR, process review and training activities; Timely submission/compliance of all Audit Reports; Any other activity as directed by Team Leaders and Department/Branch Heads.

How to Apply for SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the link available on SBI’s official website https://sbi.co.in/web/careers/current-openings. No other mode of application will be entertained. Hard copy of application and other documents need not be sent to this office. The process of registration is completed only when fee is deposited with the Bank through online mode on or before the last date. Candidates are advised to apply online well before the closing date and not to wait till the last date to avoid any technical issues.

Candidates must upload all required documents at the time of online application; failure to upload documents will result in candidature not being considered for shortlisting/interview. After expiry of the window for online application, no change/correction/modification will be allowed under any circumstances.

Documents to be Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:

  • Recent Photograph & Signature (JPEG)
  • Detailed Resume (PDF)
  • ID Proof (PDF)
  • Proof of Date of Birth (PDF)
  • Caste Certificate & PwBD Certificate (if applicable) (PDF)
  • Mandatory Educational Certificates: Degree Certificate (PDF)
  • Mandatory Certification in Forex Operations from IIBF (PDF)
  • Experience Certificates (mandatory) (PDF)
  • Preferred Certificate, if any (PDF)
  • Biodata Form (duly filled, signed & scanned - available on Bank’s career website under the advertisement) (PDF)
  • Form-16 / Offer Letter / Latest Salary Slip from current employer (PDF)
  • No Objection Certificate (NOC) from present employer (mandatory if working in Government Institute/Public Sector Unit/Public Sector Bank) (PDF)
  • Mandatory Undertaking (format available on Bank’s career page against Adv. No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/05) (PDF)

Apply Online at: https://sbi.co.in/web/careers/current-openings

Important Instructions for SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Candidates must apply online only; hard copy of application and other documents need not be sent to SBI’s office.
  • The position of Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) shall remain in Specialist cadre and will have no provision for conversion into General cadre.
  • Teaching and training experience will not be counted for eligibility.
  • Candidates with any disciplinary proceedings pending or contemplated against them as on date will not be eligible to apply; an undertaking must be uploaded at the time of online application.
  • CIBIL: Candidates who have defaulted in repayment under any lending arrangement with Banks/NBFCs/Financial Institutions including credit card dues and have not regularized/repaid their outstanding till the date of issuance of offer of recruitment by the Bank shall not be eligible for appointment.
  • Candidates serving in Govt./Quasi Govt. offices/Public Sector undertakings/Nationalized Banks must submit NOC from their employer at the time of interview, failing which their candidature will not be considered.
  • In case of multiple applications for the same post, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained; fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • OBC certificate containing the ‘non-creamy layer’ clause, issued during the period 01.04.2026 to the date of interview, must be submitted by OBC candidates if called for interview.
  • EWS candidates not in possession of “Income & Asset Certificate” for FY2025-26 on or before closure of online application date should apply under “UR Category” only.
  • Outstation candidates called for interview will be reimbursed airfare (economy class, shortest route, to-and-fro) up to a maximum of Rs. 10,000/- on submission of original flight tickets/invoice and boarding pass.
  • All changes/updates/revisions/corrigendum/results will be hosted on the Bank’s website only; no separate intimation will be issued.
  • Any legal proceedings arising out of this advertisement can be instituted only in Mumbai; Courts/Tribunals/Forums at Mumbai only shall have sole and exclusive jurisdiction.
  • Candidates are advised to keep their email ID/mobile number active till the declaration of result and issuance of call letters on final selection.
  • SBI does not endorse, authorize or associate with any external coaching platform, consultancy, individual or digital channel claiming to provide guaranteed selection or insider guidance. Candidates must rely solely on information available on SBI’s official career portal.

How to Check SBI Clerk Mains Score Card 2026?

Candidates can download their SBI Clerk Mains Score Card 2026 by following this simple process:

  2. Go to the "Careers" section and find the link for "Recruitment of Junior Associates (CRPD/CR/2025-26/06)" or "SBI Clerk Mains Score Card 2026".
  3. Click on the scorecard download link.

How to Calculate Your Score Using SBI Clerk Mains Answer Key 2026 ?

Use this formula to estimate your score:

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Ballari District Co-operative Central Bank Ltd. (Bellary DCC Bank) Manager / First Division Assistant, Second Division Assistant, Vehicle Driver and Junior Servant Notification 2026 has been released on 01 June 2026 on the official website https://www.bellarydcc.bank.in. A total of 82 vacancies have been announced through the Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026.

Candidates aged 18 to 40 years who have passed SSLC, Second PUC, Degree, and possess the prescribed educational qualifications for the respective posts are eligible to apply for these Manager / First Division Assistant, Second Division Assistant, Vehicle Driver and Junior Servant vacancies. The online application process for Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 has started on 01 June 2026 and will remain open until 30 June 2026.

Candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply online through the direct links provided below in this article.

FOLLOW US

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Ballari District Co-operative Central Bank Ltd., Hosapete (P.B. No.17, Hosapete – 583201) has officially released Recruitment Notification No. ಆಸಿ/ಸಿಬ್ಬಂದಿ/ನೇಮಕಾತಿ/819/2026-27 dated 01-06-2026 for recruitment to various posts. The recruitment is carried out in compliance with Karnataka Government Order No. ಕ.ಸ:ಕರ್.ಸಿ.ಎಸ್/ಸಿಆರ್‌ಡಿ/ಸಿಎಬಿ-5(ಡಿಸಿಬಿ)/3/2026,ಸಿ.ಆರ್.ದಿ.1 dated 31.01.2026 regarding cooperative bank recruitment. The notification contains complete details on post-wise and category-wise vacancies, eligibility criteria, educational qualifications, age limits, selection process, exam pattern, application fee, reservation rules, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying.

Download Bellary DCC Junior Assistant, Driver and More Bank Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

Bellary DCC Bank, Hosapete is recruiting 82 posts across 4 categories, last date 30 June 2026.

Organization Name Ballari District Co-operative Central Bank Ltd. (Bellary DCC Bank), Hosapete
Post Names Manager / First Division Assistant; Second Division Assistant; Vehicle Driver; Junior Servant
Total Vacancies 82
Application Mode Online only (via https://www.bellarydcc.bank.in)
Selection Mode Written Examination + Interview
Application Start Date 01-06-2026
Last Date for Application & Fee Payment 30-06-2026
Official Website https://www.bellarydcc.bank.in

Written Examination Date To be notified via SMS/Email/Bank Website
Interview Date To be notified after Written Examination result

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must possess the following educational qualifications as on the last date of application. Knowledge of Kannada is mandatory for all posts - candidates must be able to read, write, speak, and understand Kannada.

Sr. No. Post Name Educational Qualification
1 Manager / First Division Assistant 1. Must have passed a degree from any University established by law in India. 2. Must have the ability to read Kannada along with knowledge of Kannada language, writing, clear spoken Kannada, and ability to understand Kannada. 3. Must have knowledge of computer operation.
2 Second Division Assistant 1. Must have passed Second PUC or equivalent from a recognized institution, or currently studying. 2. Must have the ability to read Kannada along with knowledge of Kannada language, writing, clear spoken Kannada, and ability to understand Kannada. 3. Must have knowledge of computer operation.
3 Vehicle Driver 1. Must have passed SSLC; must be able to read Kannada as one language. 2. Must hold a valid legal driving license.
4 Junior Servant 1. Must have passed SSLC; must be able to read Kannada as one language.

Note:

  • If reserved category seats for Women / Rural Candidates / Ex-Servicemen remain unfilled after shortlisting, those vacancies will be filled from other eligible candidates in the respective category.
  • Rule 371(J) reservation: Posts reserved for Kalyana Karnataka Region are for candidates from that region. Non-371(J) refers to posts from the rest of the state (outside Kalyana Karnataka region).

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 18 years of age as on the last date of application. The following maximum age limits apply:

Sr. No. Category Maximum Age Limit
1 Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), Backward Hindus (Category-1) 40 years
2 Other Backward Classes (Categories 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B) 38 years
3 All Other Candidates (General/UR) 35 years

Age Relaxation (Over and above the above limits):

  • Ex-Servicemen: Age equivalent to the period of military service rendered (as per Government norms).
  • Differently-Abled (Physically Challenged) Candidates: 10 years additional relaxation.
  • Widowed Women: 10 years additional (candidate must upload a certificate from the Tahsildar confirming widowhood and non-remarriage).

Special Note: As per Government Notification No. ಸಿ.ಆ.ಸು.ಇ:2062:ಸೇನೆನಿ 2025, Bengaluru dated 29.01.2026, all category candidates who were participating in the selection process based on the age schedule as on the notified maximum age limit are to be provided 5 years of one-time age relaxation (As a One Time Measure) for participating in recruitment processes. Candidates who applied under the previous notification dated 06.04.2026 and were within the age limit are considered eligible for the current notification's age limit as on 30.06.2026.

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Category Application Fee
General / Other Categories (2A, 2B, 3A, 3B) Rs. 1,000/- + Gateway charges (as applicable)
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe / Category-1 / Differently-Abled / Ex-Servicemen Rs. 500/- + Gateway charges (as applicable)

Important Fee Rules:

  • Application and fee payment start date: 01-06-2026
  • Last date for application and fee payment: 30-06-2026
  • Payment mode: Online only - Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, UPI, or Google Pay; DD/Postal Order/Money Order/any other mode is NOT accepted.
  • Fee once paid will NOT be refunded under any circumstances.
  • Each post requires a separate application and separate fee payment.

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

(A) Manager / First Division Assistant

Step 1 - Written Examination + Interview: Vacancy filling is done through written examination and personal interview.

Step 2 - Shortlisting for Written Exam: Online applications are screened based on prescribed educational qualifications and required documents. Eligible candidates are called for written examination conducted by an external government-authorized body. Based on marks scored in the prescribed written examination, an eligibility list is prepared and candidates are called for interview in a 1:5 ratio (15 marks minimum to qualify for interview).

Step 3 - Merit List: Written exam marks (minimum 85 out of 100 to qualify) combined with interview marks are totaled to prepare a final merit list (Merit List), following reservation norms for final appointment.

Written Examination Pattern for Manager / First Division Assistant - 200 Marks, Duration: 2 Hours, 1 Mark per Question:

Sr. No. Subject Marks
1 Kannada Language 50
2 General English Language 25
3 General Knowledge 25
4 Co-operative Subject 50
5 Indian Constitution 25
6 Banking, Social Economics Activities and Consumer Affairs 25
Total   200

(B) Second Division Assistant

Step 1 - Written Examination + Interview: Vacancy filling is done through written examination and personal interview.

Step 2 - Shortlisting: Same process as Manager post - online applications screened, eligible candidates called for written exam by external body, merit list prepared in 1:5 ratio (15 marks minimum for interview).

Step 3 - Merit List: Written exam marks (minimum 85 to qualify) combined with interview marks totaled to prepare final Merit List, following reservation norms.

Written Examination Pattern for Second Division Assistant - 200 Marks, Duration: 2 Hours, 1 Mark per Question:

Sr. No. Subject Marks
1 Kannada Language 50
2 General English Language 25
3 General Knowledge 25
4 Co-operative Subject 50
5 Indian Constitution 25
6 Banking, Social Economics Activities and Consumer Affairs 25
Total   200

(C) Vehicle Driver

Step 1 - Written Examination + Interview: Selection is through written examination and personal interview.

Step 2 - Shortlisting: Online applications screened based on prescribed qualifications and required documents; eligible candidates called for written exam by external body in 1:5 ratio (15 marks minimum for interview).

Step 3 - Merit List: Written exam marks (minimum 85) combined with interview marks for final Merit List; reservation norms followed.

Written Examination Pattern for Vehicle Driver - 100 Marks, Duration: 1 Hour, 1 Mark per Question:

Sr. No. Subject Marks
1 Kannada Language 50
2 General Knowledge 50
Total   100

(D) Junior Servant

Step 1 - Written Examination + Interview: Selection is through written examination and personal interview.

Step 2 - Shortlisting: Online applications screened; eligible candidates called for written exam by external body in 1:5 ratio (15 marks minimum for interview).

Step 3 - Merit List: Written exam marks (minimum 85) combined with interview marks for final Merit List; reservation norms followed.

Written Examination Pattern for Junior Servant - 100 Marks, Duration: 1 Hour, 1 Mark per Question:

Sr. No. Subject Marks
1 Kannada Language 50
2 General Knowledge 50
Total   100

Note: Question papers for all posts will be in Kannada and English (Kannada language and General English section excepted). Each question carries 1 mark; there is NO negative marking. Candidates scoring equal marks at written exam will be given preference to older candidates as per Karnataka State Government recruitment rules.

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - How to Apply

Candidates must apply Online only through the Bank's official website. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before filling the application, candidates must mandatorily read the notification and instructions carefully.

Step-by-Step Application Process:

Step 1: Visit the Bank's official website https://www.bellarydcc.bank.in and open the link "BDCC RECRUITMENT 2026" on a desktop/laptop computer. Read the notification and instructions carefully before filling the application.

  • After clicking the registration box, a confirmation check box will appear for candidates who have previously applied and paid the fee. Those candidates must re-submit the application with the same registration number clearly mentioned, upload documents, and re-submit.
  • A check box will appear: "Are you re-applying after already submitting an application and fee payment?" If Yes, mention the previous application registration number clearly, upload documents, and re-submit the application.

Step 2: Visit http://www.bellarydcc.bank.in, register and log in. Enter your SSLC marks, name, date of birth, disability status, and other required details. Verify mobile number and email ID via OTP. Register successfully.

  • Candidates who previously applied and downloaded the application under the earlier notification must register again with a new registration number and re-upload documents per the new requirements. Fee once paid for the earlier application is not refunded, and no separate fee is required again for that post.

Step 3: Use your registration number and date of birth as login credentials. Review and edit all details and qualification information if needed. Preview the application carefully before confirming. Once submitted, no information can be changed.

Step 4: Upload Photo and Signature. The candidate must upload their passport-size photograph on a blank white paper with signature below using a black Sketch/Marker pen; scan and upload. Applications without photo/signature or incomplete applications will be rejected.

  • After uploading photo and signature, upload all required SSLC marks/reservation-related documents with self-attestation, then submit.

Step 5: After submitting, pay the application fee and applicable gateway charges through the Payment Gateway link.

Photo Specifications:

  • File size: Less than 100 KB
  • DPI Setting: 100 DPI
  • Quality: 100%
  • Color: Color
  • Format: JPG/JPEG
  • Photo size: 35mm × 45mm; Signature below: 35mm × 20mm

Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026 - Required Documents

The following documents must be uploaded online at the time of application and must be valid as on the last date of application. Originals must be produced at the time of verification.

For All Posts:

  1. Educational qualification certificates or mark sheets for the prescribed qualification (all years/semesters).
  2. SSLC marks card / School attendance certificate or Cumulative Record Extract (for date of birth proof).
  3. Category/reservation certificate as applicable.

For Reservation / Age Relaxation Certificates:

  1. Ex-Servicemen: Discharge certificate and Pension/Gratuity certificate from the authorized authority.
  2. SC/ST (Category A, B, C) / ST-Pangada candidates: Category certificates in the prescribed Government format - SC/ST in Form-D/E/F as applicable; Category-1 candidates in Form-E; 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B candidates in Form-F issued by concerned Tahsildar.
  3. Candidates currently employed: Service certificate from employer and No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the relevant recruitment authority.
  4. Kalyana Karnataka Region candidates: Eligibility proof certificate as per Annexure-2 from the authorized sub-divisional authority.
  5. Rural candidates: Certificate in Form-1 and General candidates in Form-1 and Form-2 from the concerned Tahsildar.
  6. Differently-Abled candidates: Medical certificate from the District-level Medical Board in the prescribed Government format.
  7. Manager / First Division Assistant post: All year/semester mark sheets of the degree.
  8. Second Division Assistant post: Second PUC mark sheets / current student proof.
  9. Vehicle Driver post: SSLC marks card and valid legal driving license.
  10. Junior Servant post: SSLC marks card.
  11. Candidates applying for any post must mandatorily upload a self-portrait passport-size photograph with signature as described above.
  12. Candidates currently working anywhere must upload service certificate from the head of that organization and a No Objection Certificate.
  13. Rural / Ex-Servicemen / Kannada Medium / Differently-Abled category claimants must upload the competent authority-issued certificate.

Special Instructions for Previously Applied Candidates

  • Candidates who had applied and paid the fee under Bank Recruitment Notification No. 12:2026–27 dated 06.04.2026 and who wish to apply for the same posts in the current notification must mandatorily re-apply online. They must clearly mention the previous registration number and re-upload all required documents. No separate fee payment is required for those posts.

  • Candidates who had applied and downloaded the application under the 06.04.2026 notification must register freshly with a new registration number, re-submit, and upload documents as per current requirements. Once Application Re-submit is done, it cannot be re-edited and fees paid will not be refunded. Each post still requires a separate application; one Application Re-submit per post is allowed.
  • Information regarding re-application will be communicated via SMS and email to the registered mobile number and email ID of candidates who had previously applied and downloaded their application under the earlier notification.

Caste Reservation Certificate Requirements

For candidates claiming reservation benefits:

  • SC (Category A, B, C) / ST / Pangada candidates: Upload certificate in Form-D
  • Category-1 candidates: Upload certificate in Form-E
  • Category-2A, 2B, 3A, 3B candidates: Upload certificate in Form-F, obtained from the concerned Tahsildar; these certificates for Backward Hindu categories must be valid within 5 years from the date of application.

Rural Candidate Reservation:

  • Rural candidates must have studied from Class 1 to Class 10 in a village (rural area) under the current rules; certificate must be current and valid.
  • SC/ST/Pangada candidates claiming Rural reservation must submit Form-2 rural reservation certificate.
  • General category candidates claiming rural reservation must submit Form-1 rural certificate along with proof that they are not otherwise listed as urban.
  • General category, SC/ST/Pangada, Category-1, 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B candidates who fail to submit the required rural reservation certificate will be treated as ineligible for rural reservation.
  • Note: Certificate in Form-2 must be obtained from the Headmaster of the school where the candidate studied and attested by the concerned field education officer.

Kannada Medium Candidates:

  • Candidates claiming Kannada Medium reservation must submit a certificate from the Headmaster of the school where they studied Kannada medium along with date of school's issue, signed certificate, and must be held valid as on the last date of application. Original copies must be produced at verification. If not produced, the Kannada Medium reservation will be cancelled.

Scheme-Deprived (Yojane Nirashritha) Candidates:

  • Must produce a certificate from the concerned Tahsildar in the prescribed format as per current rules. Certificate must be valid up to the last date of application and must be current.

Ex-Servicemen:

  • Ex-Serviceman means a person who has served in any rank (combatant or non-combatant) in the Central Armed Forces (Army, Navy, Air Force) - excluding persons who served in Defence Security Corps, General Reserve Engineering Force, Lok Sahayak Sena, and Para-Military Forces.
  • Family members (spouse/husband/wife and children, including adopted children) of persons who died while serving in Central Armed Forces are eligible for Ex-Servicemen reservation; however, age relaxation will not be provided to them.
  • Eligible cases for Ex-Servicemen reservation:
    • Retired voluntarily from such service after receiving pension; OR
    • Released from military service on medical grounds with medical and physical disability pension; OR
    • Released from service due to reduction of personnel; OR
    • Released from service after completing specific service and receiving gratuity, after fulfilling period of service - including: persons retired from continuous service, persons with physical disability due to military service, and gazette prize winners.
  • Persons currently re-employed in government service after retiring from military service must apply for military reservation through their current service NOC before appointment; they are allowed to enjoy all benefits available to ex-servicemen during re-employment, but the bank will not be responsible if they are not matched with the post. They must submit their discharge certificate before applying.
  • Ex-servicemen applicants, persons dependent on ex-servicemen who were killed or permanently disabled while in service - (spouse/husband/wife and children, including adopted children) - are eligible for Ex-Servicemen reservation benefits. Age relaxation will not be provided to dependents. They must submit the discharge certificate and permanence/gratuity certificate; at the time of original document verification, original copies must be produced.

Differently-Abled (Physically Challenged) Candidates:

  • Only candidates with a minimum of 40% benchmark disability certified by the District-level Medical Board in the prescribed Government format (NOT in any other format) are eligible for differently-abled reservation. Certificate must be issued in the prescribed Government notification format.
  • Differently-Abled candidates who require a scribe for the written examination must upload a special medical certificate from the Medical Board stating that they are unable to write due to physical disability.
  • The scribe requirement must be indicated at the time of application in the specified field in the online application; only when required, the candidate may request a scribe from the examination centre authority.

Who Should NOT Apply for Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates who do not meet the prescribed educational qualifications (degree for Manager/FDA; Second PUC for SDA; SSLC for Driver and Junior Servant) as on the last date of application.

  • Candidates who cannot read, write, speak, and understand Kannada - knowledge of Kannada is mandatory for all posts.
  • Candidates below 18 years of age or above the maximum age limit applicable to their category.
  • OBC (Category-2A/2B/3A/3B) Creamy Layer candidates applying under reserved categories - only non-Creamy Layer OBC candidates are eligible for reservation.
  • Candidates with less than 40% disability who apply for Differently-Abled reservation - only 40%+ benchmark disability qualifies.
  • Vehicle Driver candidates who do not hold a valid legal driving license.
  • Candidates currently employed in any organization who cannot produce an NOC from their employer.
  • Candidates who obtained degrees or certificates from institutions declared fake or derecognized by competent educational authorities - such applications will be rejected at any stage.
  • Candidates who provide false information or suppress facts - such candidates are liable for criminal prosecution and disqualification at any stage of the selection process.
  • Persons who are members of or active in any banned organization, or who have attempted to overthrow the Government of India, or who have been convicted under any law, or who have taken part in activities against the sovereignty or integrity of India, or who have been dismissed from any government service.
  • Candidates with mental illness or mental disability.
  • A male candidate who has more than one living wife, and a woman candidate who has married a man who already has another living wife, are not eligible.

Common Mistakes / What to Check Before Applying Bellary DCC Bank Recruitment 2026?

  • Applying for the wrong post for which you do not meet the educational qualification.

  • Selecting the wrong category (General/SC/ST/OBC/EWS) - categories cannot be changed after submission; mismatched certificates lead to cancellation.
  • OBC (Backward Hindu 2A/2B/3A/3B) candidates from Creamy Layer applying under OBC reservation instead of General category.
  • Not uploading all mandatory documents at the time of online application - incomplete applications will be rejected outright.
  • Uploading blurred, illegible, or incorrect photographs, signatures, or certificates - leading to rejection at document verification stage.
  • Using an inactive or wrong mobile number and email ID - exam dates, interview schedules, and admit cards are communicated only through SMS and email.
  • Submitting multiple applications for the same post without following the proper Re-submit process - only the last valid application is considered and fees for others are forfeited.
  • Not reading the separate instructions for candidates who applied under the earlier notification (06.04.2026) - they must re-apply with their old registration number and need not pay fee again for the same post.
  • Not noting that each post requires a separate application and separate fee - applying for multiple posts with a single application/fee is not permitted.
  • Paying the fee through DD/Postal Order/Money Order or any offline mode - only online payment (Net Banking/Credit Card/Debit Card/UPI/Google Pay) is accepted.
  • Not keeping originals of all uploaded documents ready for verification - originals must be produced at the time of interview/verification.
  • Ignoring the minimum qualifying mark requirement: candidates must score a minimum of 85 out of 100 (or 200) in the written examination to qualify for the interview.

Important Instructions for Bellary DCC Bank  Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Applications must be submitted Online only at https://www.bellarydcc.bank.in; no other mode is accepted.

  • Candidates must mandatorily keep Kannada language proficiency - it is essential for all posts.
  • Information provided by candidates at the time of application regarding category, date of birth, educational qualification, age relaxation, etc., can only be for consideration purposes and will not be changed later; no request for change will be entertained afterward.
  • Incomplete applications, applications without photo/signature, or applications with incorrect information will be summarily rejected.
  • Candidates must self-attest all documents and mandatorily upload them online.
  • Candidates must keep a printout of the submitted application for their own reference.
  • Admit cards for the written examination and date/venue/time of interview will be communicated via SMS, email, or the Bank's website; admit cards will NOT be sent by post.
  • The Bank/external body reserves the right to advance, postpone, cancel, and change the rules of the recruitment process at any time without giving any reason.
  • The decision of the Bank's Board/Staff Recruitment Committee shall be final in all matters relating to recruitment; no correspondence will be entertained.
  • Candidates must serve a probation period of 2 years after selection.
  • Selected candidates will be provided security deposit and personal bond as informed by the Bank; they must sign a contract and pay the prescribed security deposit on joining.
  • This recruitment is subject to directions and orders of the Karnataka Government / High Court during the recruitment notification period.
  • Canvassing in any form will result in disqualification.
  • No correspondence regarding recruitment will be entertained from any candidate after the process has concluded.

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process consists of an Online Examination followed by an Interview. Merely satisfying the eligibility norm does not entitle a candidate to be called for the test or interview. Candidates qualified in the online test will be called for interview to the extent of 4 times the number of vacancies. Final merit will be based on the combined performance in Online Test and Interview in the ratio of 70:30. In case of equal marks, candidates senior in age will be placed higher in the merit list.

How to Apply for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026

Candidates can apply only online from 30-04-2026 to 07-06-2026 at the link https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/cbiagmapr26/. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates should scan their photograph (200x230 pixels, 20-50 KB), signature (140x60 pixels, 10-20 KB), left thumb impression (240x240 pixels, 20-50 KB), and handwritten declaration (800x400 pixels, 50-100 KB). A valid personal email ID and contact number must be kept active till completion of the recruitment process. Not more than one application should be submitted by any candidate. In case of multiple applications, only the latest valid (completed) application will be retained.

An indemnity bond of INR 3,00,000/- must be executed by the selected candidate to indemnify the Bank in case such candidate leaves the service within 3 years of selection. Candidates must have a minimum CIC (Credit Information Company) score of 650 with no credit default or financial indiscipline as on the date of document verification.

Documents to be Produced at the Time of Interview (Original + Self-attested copies):

  • Original Marks Sheet & Provisional Certificate / Degree Certificate issued from University
  • Experience Certificates as per eligibility requirements
  • Caste / PwBD (Divyangjan) / Caste Validity Certificate (wherever applicable)
  • OBC Certificate with Non-Creamy Layer clause (for OBC candidates)
  • EWS Income and Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates)
  • No Objection Certificate from employer (for candidates serving in Govt./PSU/Nationalized Banks)
  • Colour passport size photograph (same as uploaded in application form)

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/cbiagmapr26/

Important Instructions for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only one application per candidate - multiple applications will result in forfeiture of fee paid for other registrations.
  • Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn and fee once paid is neither refunded nor held in reserve.
  • Candidates must ensure eligibility before applying - the Bank reserves the right to reject applications at any stage.
  • Minimum CIC score of 650 is mandatory with no credit default in the credit report.
  • Indemnity bond of INR 3,00,000/- must be executed if selected (valid for 3 years of service).
  • Probation period of 2 years from the date of joining.
  • Initial posting will be at Mumbai and thereafter transferable anywhere in India as per bank's transfer policy.
  • Government/PSU/Nationalized Bank employees must produce NOC from employer at the time of interview.
  • OBC candidates in 'Creamy Layer' will be treated as 'General Category'.
  • No Travelling Allowance is payable to candidates called for interview. However, unemployed SC/ST candidates will be paid 2nd Class Rail/Ordinary Bus fare on production of travel evidence.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Any resulting dispute shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of Courts situated at Mumbai.
  • Candidates are advised to regularly visit the Bank's website www.centralbank.bank.in for updates under Career section.
  • All decisions of the Bank regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding on all candidates.

Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 4 Deputy Manager (Information Security) vacancies have been announced under Advertisement No. HRM/DM/2026-27/03. Reservations are applicable as per prevailing Government of India guidelines. The Bank reserves the right to cancel/restrict/curtail/enlarge the recruitment process or revise the number of vacancies without notice.

Position UR OBC (NCL) Total
Deputy Manager (Information Security) 03 01 04

Abbreviations: UR – Unreserved, OBC (NCL) – Other Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer). Note: Reservations for SC/ST/EWS/PwBD as applicable per prevailing GoI guidelines. Selected candidates will be posted anywhere in India as per Bank's requirement and may be transferred to offices outside India with consent.

Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions (qualification, experience, and age) as on the prescribed dates. Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates having completed their graduation/post-graduation through correspondence/part-time/open university/open learning/external/distance learning are NOT eligible. For CGPA-based grading, candidates must provide a conversion letter from the Head of Institute/Controller of Examination if the equivalent percentage is not mentioned on the marksheet/certificate.

Criteria Details
Nationality Indian Citizens only.
Essential Academic Qualification Minimum 60% aggregate marks / equivalent CGPA in Full-time B.E./B.Tech./M.Tech./M.E./M.C.A. in Computer Science / Information Technology / Electronics & Communication from a University/Institute recognized/approved by Government/AICTE/UGC.
Essential Experience Minimum 1 year of experience in a Scheduled Commercial Bank or All India Financial Institution or any other organization of repute, in Information Security functions.
Preferred Certifications Preference given to candidates possessing any one of: CISA / CISM / CISSP / OSCP / CEH / CISP / CRTP / CCNA / CCNP / ISO 27001:2022 LA/LI / CompTIA Security+.
Additional Skills Excellent communication skills and proficiency in computers and information technology.
Not Eligible Candidates who completed graduation/post-graduation through correspondence/part-time/open university/open learning/external/distance learning are not eligible.
OBC Non-Creamy Layer OBC certificate must specifically contain a clause of non-creamy layer exclusion from GoI reservation benefits. Certificate must be valid as on last date of applying (issued within one year prior to last date). Caste name must tally letter by letter with Central Government list. OBC Creamy Layer candidates and those whose caste is not in Central List must apply as General.
Government Employees Candidates already in service must produce a proper relieving letter/discharge certificate in original from present employer at the time of joining, failing which they will not be allowed to join.
Service Bond Selected candidates must execute a personal bond of Rs. 3 lakh to serve the Bank for a minimum period of 5 years from date of joining.

Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be not below 21 years and not above 28 years as on 01-05-2026. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories as per Government of India guidelines. OBC (Creamy Layer) candidates must apply under General category and are not entitled to age relaxation.

Sr. No. Category Maximum Age (as on 01-05-2026)
1 General (UR) / EWS 28 years
2 OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 31 years (3 years relaxation)
3 Persons with Disabilities (PwBDs) 10 years relaxation over General limit
4 Ex-Servicemen 5 years relaxation (as per DOPT circular No. 36034/2/2013 dated 08-04-2013, for Group A direct recruitment through All India Competitive Examination)

Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fees and intimation charges are non-refundable. The process of registration is complete only when fee is deposited through online mode on or before the last date i.e. 14-06-2026. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid will not be refunded even if the applicant is not found eligible.

Category Application Fee / Intimation Charges
General / OBC (NCL) Rs. 600/- (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD / EWS / Female candidates (all communities) Rs. 100/- (Intimation Charges only - Non-Refundable)

Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be based on Written Test followed by Personal Interview. Candidates shortlisted based on written test performance will be called for Personal Interview. Final selection will be based on overall performance in both stages. Written Examination carries 70% weightage and Personal Interview carries 30% weightage in the final merit list. There will be no sectional cut-off in the written test. Exim Bank will analyse responses to detect patterns of similarity - if copying is inferred, candidature will be cancelled.

Stage 1 - Written Test (Professional Knowledge - Objective Type)

Name of the Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
Professional Knowledge - Objective (Part I: Information Technology and Cyber Security) 100 100 2 Hours 30 Minutes

Candidates may be required to be at the venue for about 4 hours (including time for document verification, instructions, etc.). No request for change of centre/venue/date/session will be entertained. Examination centres: Kolkata, Mumbai, New Delhi, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad (may change if sufficient candidates not available). Personal Interviews will be conducted only in Mumbai and New Delhi. Written test carries 70% weightage; Interview carries 30% weightage in final merit.

Stage 2 - Personal Interview

Candidates shortlisted on the basis of written test performance will be called for Personal Interview at Mumbai or New Delhi. Candidates must produce original degree certificates, caste, experience and other relevant certificates at the time of interview. Candidates already in service must carry a proper relieving letter/discharge certificate from their present employer at the time of joining.

Exim Bank Deputy Manager (Information Security) Recruitment 2026 - Pre-Recruitment Training

A pre-recruitment training will be organized for candidates from reserved categories. Details of the training and its mode of delivery (online/offline) will be intimated to candidates who opt for it through the online application form. Details will be shared after the last date of registration.

Who should NOT apply for Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates who do not hold a full-time B.E./B.Tech./M.Tech./M.E./M.C.A. in Computer Science/IT/Electronics & Communication - degrees from other disciplines or part-time/distance/correspondence courses are strictly ineligible.
  • Candidates with aggregate marks below 60% in their qualifying degree - the 60% minimum is a hard eligibility condition.
  • Candidates who do not have at least 1 year of post-qualification experience in Information Security functions in a Scheduled Commercial Bank, All India Financial Institution, or other organization of repute - experience in unrelated IT roles does not qualify.
  • Candidates outside the age band of 21 to 28 years as on 01-05-2026 (except those entitled to OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen relaxation).
  • OBC Creamy Layer candidates expecting OBC reservation or age relaxation - they must apply as General.
  • Candidates not willing to serve anywhere in India - posting is at the Bank's requirement at any location in India including transfers to overseas offices with consent.
  • Candidates who cannot commit to the 5-year service bond of Rs. 3 lakh - this bond is mandatory and must be executed at the time of joining.
  • Candidates currently in government service who cannot produce a proper relieving letter/discharge certificate from their employer at the time of joining - they will not be allowed to join without it.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026?

  • Uploading a signature in CAPITAL LETTERS - capital letter signatures are not accepted and will lead to rejection.
  • Not writing the handwritten declaration personally in English - if written by anyone else or in any other language, the application will be declared invalid.
  • Not uploading a live photograph via webcam/mobile at the time of filling the application - this is mandatory in addition to the static scanned photograph.
  • Uploading blurry, smudged, or unclear photograph, signature, left thumb impression, or handwritten declaration - such applications may be rejected; candidates should preview all uploads before final submission.
  • Not cross-checking that the name in the application exactly matches the name on the photo identity proof - any mismatch leads to denial of entry at the examination/interview; female candidates who have changed name post-marriage must be especially careful.
  • Clicking Back or Refresh during online fee payment - this may cause double charges; wait for server intimation after payment submission.
  • Not taking a printout of the e-Receipt and online application form after fee payment - this is required for future reference.
  • Not keeping the email ID and mobile number active till the end of the recruitment process - all communications including admit card, call letters, and results are sent only via email/SMS.
  • Not furnishing the CGPA conversion formula/letter from University/Institute if degree is CGPA-based - without this, the equivalent percentage cannot be verified and candidature may be rejected.
  • Waiting till the last date to apply - the Bank is not responsible for website outages or connectivity issues; candidates must apply well before 14-06-2026.

How to Apply for Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must apply online only through the link available on the Bank's Careers page at https://www.eximbankindia.in/careers. The application link will be live from 26-05-2026. No other means or mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates must scan and keep ready all required documents. The application registration is complete only when fee is successfully paid online on or before 14-06-2026.

Documents to be Scanned and Uploaded before applying:

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 200×230 pixels preferred; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG) - light/white background, no caps, dark glasses or religious headwear covering face
  • Live photograph - to be captured via webcam or mobile phone QR scan at the time of filling the application form (mandatory in addition to static photo)
  • Signature with Black Ink on white paper (140×60 pixels preferred; 10 KB–20 KB; JPEG/JPG) - NOT in CAPITAL LETTERS
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with Black or Blue ink (240×240 pixels in 200 DPI preferred; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG/JPG)
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with Black ink (800×400 pixels in 200 DPI preferred; 50 KB–100 KB; JPEG/JPG) - in candidate's own handwriting only
  • Consolidated Mark Sheets of Graduation & Post-Graduation (PDF; 20 KB–500 KB)
  • Graduation & Post-Graduation Degree Certificate (PDF; 20 KB–500 KB)
  • Caste Certificate - if applicable (PDF; 20 KB–500 KB)

Documents to be Produced in Original at the time of Written Examination / Interview:

  • Call Letter (downloaded from Bank's website) with original and photocopy of currently valid photo identity proof bearing exactly the same name as on the call letter - valid IDs: PAN Card, Passport, Permanent Driving License, Voter's Card, Bank Passbook with photograph, Photo identity proof issued by Gazetted Officer on official letterhead, Photo identity proof issued by People's Representative on official letterhead, valid College/University Identity Card, Aadhaar Card/E-Aadhaar with photograph, Employee ID, or Bar Council Identity Card. (Note: Ration Card and Learner's Driving License are NOT valid.)
  • Original degree certificates, caste certificate, experience certificates, and any other relevant certificates (at the time of interview)
  • In case of name change - original Gazette notification, original marriage certificate, or affidavit in original
  • CGPA conversion letter from Head of Institute/Controller of Examination (if CGPA-based grading)
  • Scribe undertaking and disability/medical certificate (for PwBD candidates requesting scribe facility - must be registered for scribe and compensatory time in online application form)

The handwritten declaration text is: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."

Candidates are advised to take a printout of the system-generated online application form after registration. No change/alteration is possible after clicking COMPLETE REGISTRATION. Any legal proceedings can be instituted only in Mumbai - Mumbai courts/tribunals/forums shall have sole and exclusive jurisdiction.

Apply Online at: https://www.eximbankindia.in/careers

Important Instructions for Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Application link will be live from 26-05-2026 and closes on 14-06-2026 - apply well in advance without waiting for the last date.
  • Registration is complete only when fee is paid online on or before 14-06-2026. Fee once paid is non-refundable under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must carry the call letter along with original and a photocopy of currently valid photo identity proof to the examination and interview - without these, entry will not be permitted.
  • The name on the call letter must exactly match the name on the photo identity proof - female candidates who have changed name post-marriage must note this carefully.
  • Candidates reporting late after the reporting time on the call letter will NOT be permitted to take the examination.
  • No request for change of examination centre/venue/date/session will be entertained.
  • Mobile phones (even in switched-off mode), pagers, or any electronic communication devices are strictly prohibited in the examination/interview hall - violation leads to disqualification and possible debarment from future Exim Bank recruitments.
  • Exim Bank will analyse response patterns - if response sharing is detected, candidature will be cancelled and results withheld.
  • Pre-recruitment training will be organized for reserved category candidates who opt for it in the online application form.
  • PwBD candidates requiring a scribe must arrange their own scribe at their own cost and must indicate the requirement in the online application form - subsequent requests may not be entertained. Eligible PwBD/locomotor disability/visually impaired candidates get 20 minutes of compensatory time per hour.
  • Selected candidates must execute a personal bond of Rs. 3 lakh to serve the Bank for a minimum of 5 years from date of joining.
  • Selected candidates will be posted anywhere in India as per Bank's requirement and may be transferred to overseas offices with consent.
  • Candidates already in service must produce a relieving letter/discharge certificate from present employer at the time of joining - without it, joining will not be permitted.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • All matters regarding eligibility, selection process, results, etc. - the Bank's decision is final and binding. No correspondence will be entertained from ineligible or non-selected candidates.
  • Legal proceedings can only be instituted in Mumbai - Mumbai courts/tribunals/forums have sole and exclusive jurisdiction.

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Pattern 2026

Understanding the exam pattern is crucial for effective preparation. Here's what candidates need to know about the RRB NTPC Undergraduate examination:

  • Exam Mode: Online Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Total Marks: 100
  • Number of Questions: 100
  • Duration: 90 Minutes
  • Negative Marking: Yes (1/3rd mark deducted for each wrong answer)
  • Subjects/Sections: General Awareness (40 Qs/40 Marks), Mathematics (30 Qs/30 Marks), General Intelligence and Reasoning (30 Qs/30 Marks)

Preparation Tips for RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam

With the exam date announced, candidates should focus on smart preparation strategies:

1. Create a Study Schedule: Divide your time wisely among all subjects based on your strengths and weaknesses.

2. Focus on Previous Year Papers: Solve at least 5-10 previous year question papers to understand the pattern and difficulty level.

3. Take Mock Tests: Regular mock tests will help you manage time and identify areas needing improvement.

4. Revise Regularly: Make short notes and revise them daily, especially for GK and current affairs.

5. Stay Updated: Keep yourself updated with current affairs, especially related to Indian Railways and general awareness.

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Admit Card 2026 - When to Download?

The RRB will release the admit card for NTPC Undergraduate examination approximately 4 days before the exam date. Candidates can download their hall tickets from the official website using their registration number and password. City Intimation Slip will be available 10 days before the exam.

Documents Required on Exam Day:

  • Printed copy of Admit Card
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhar Card/PAN Card/Driving License/Voter ID)
  • Recent passport-size photographs
  • Required stationery items (as per instructions)
  • Original Aadhaar or e-Aadhaar (biometric verification mandatory)

What After the RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam?

After successfully appearing in the RRB NTPC Undergraduate examination, candidates should:

1. Result Declaration: The result is typically declared after the completion of all stages. Results will be available on the official website.

2. Answer Key: Preliminary answer keys may be released after the exam. Candidates can raise objections if any discrepancy is found.

3. Next Stage: Qualified candidates will be called for CBT-2, Typing Skill Test (for applicable posts), Document Verification, and Medical Examination.

HPRCA Group Instructor Answer Key 2026 - Download PDF, Response Sheet & Objection Link

The Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA) has officially announced the release of the Group Instructor Answer Key 2026 on 02 June 2026. The organization has made the provisional answer keys available on its official website hprca.hp.gov.in and has also provided candidates with the opportunity to raise objections against any incorrect answer.

Candidates who had appeared for the Group Instructor examination conducted on 17 October 2025 are now eligible to download the provisional answer key and estimate their expected scores ahead of the final results.

Candidates who disagree with any answer can submit their objections through the official website within the prescribed objection period mentioned in the notification. The HPRCA Group Instructor Answer Key 2026 PDF can be downloaded directly from the link given below without the need for any registration number or password.

HPRCA Group Instructor Answer Key 2026 - Quick Overview

Organization Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA)
Post Name Group Instructor
Answer Key Status Released
Release Date 02 June 2026
Official Website hprca.hp.gov.in

Direct Link: Download HPRCA Group Instructor Answer Key 2026

How to Download HPRCA Group Instructor Answer Key 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your answer key:

  1. Visit the official website: hprca.hp.gov.in
  2. Navigate to the "Answer Key" or "Recruitment" section
  3. Click on "HPRCA Group Instructor Answer Key 2026" link
  4. Log in using your Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth
  5. Your answer key with recorded responses will appear on screen
  6. Download and save the PDF for future reference

How to Raise Objection Against HPRCA Group Instructor Answer Key 2026?

If you disagree with any answer in the provisional key, follow these steps:

  1. Log in to your candidate portal on hprca.hp.gov.in
  2. Go to the "Challenge/Objection" section
  3. Select the question(s) you want to challenge
  4. Upload supporting documents or references (if required)
  5. Pay the objection fee (₹XX per question, refundable if accepted)
  6. Submit before the deadline

Important: Objections submitted after the deadline will NOT be considered.

Use this formula to estimate your score:

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) - (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

Marking Scheme for Group Instructor Exam:

  • Correct Answer: +X marks
  • Wrong Answer: -X mark (negative marking)
  • Unattempted: 0 marks
